declarations.doc_comment: Checks if a value is a JSON-RPC error response (has error + id).
declarations.type_signature: (message: unknown): message is { [x: string]: unknown; jsonrpc: "2.0"; id: string | number | null; error: { [x: string]: unknown; code: -32700 | -32600 | -32601 | -32602 | -32603 | (number & $brand<"JsonrpcServerErrorCode">); message: string; data?: unknown; }; }

declarations.name: to_jsonrpc_message_id
declarations.doc_comment:
Extracts a JSON-RPC request id from a message or raw value.
Returns `null` if no valid id can be extracted.

declarations.name: to_jsonrpc_params
declarations.doc_comment:
Normalizes input to JSON-RPC params format.
Returns `undefined` for null/undefined, wraps primitives in `{value}`.

declarations.name: to_jsonrpc_result
declarations.doc_comment:
Normalizes output to JSON-RPC result format.
Returns empty object for null/undefined, wraps primitives in `{value}`.

modules.module_comment:
JSON-RPC message builders, type guards, and converters.

Used by the SAES runtime (ActionEvent, ActionPeer, transports) and
the RPC endpoint dispatcher. Complements `http/jsonrpc.ts` (schemas) and
`http/jsonrpc_errors.ts` (error infrastructure).

declarations.name: JsonrpcRequestId
declarations.doc_comment: A uniquely identifying id for a request in JSON-RPC. Like MCP, excludes null.

declarations.name: JsonrpcMethod
declarations.doc_comment: A JSON-RPC method name.

declarations.name: JsonrpcProgressToken
declarations.doc_comment: A progress token, used to associate progress notifications with the original request.

declarations.name: JsonrpcMcpMeta
declarations.doc_comment: MCP metadata object — loose to allow additional properties and `.extend`.

declarations.name: JsonrpcRequestParamsMeta
declarations.doc_comment: Request params metadata — extends MCP meta with optional progress token.

declarations.name: JsonrpcRequestParams
declarations.doc_comment: Request params — loose object. Per-action schemas validate `_meta` content.

declarations.name: JsonrpcNotificationParams
declarations.doc_comment: Notification params — loose object. Per-action schemas validate `_meta` content.

declarations.name: JsonrpcResult
declarations.doc_comment:
Result — any JSON value per JSON-RPC 2.0 §5. Per-action `spec.output`
is the actual contract; the envelope only asserts presence + JSON-ness.

`z.json()` is required (not implicitly optional like `z.unknown()` /
`z.any()`, which would let an error envelope `{jsonrpc, id, error}`
parse successfully against the success envelope and break union
discrimination).

declarations.name: JsonrpcRequest
declarations.doc_comment: A request that expects a response.
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ jsonrpc: ZodLiteral<"2.0">; id: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodString, ZodNumber]>; method: ZodString; params: ZodOptional<ZodObject<{}, $loose>>; }, $loose>

declarations.name: JsonrpcNotification
declarations.doc_comment: A notification which does not expect a response.
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ jsonrpc: ZodLiteral<"2.0">; method: ZodString; params: ZodOptional<ZodObject<{}, $loose>>; }, $loose>

declarations.name: JsonrpcResponse
declarations.doc_comment: A successful (non-error) response to a request.
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ jsonrpc: ZodLiteral<"2.0">; id: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodString, ZodNumber]>; result: ZodJSONSchema; }, $loose>

declarations.name: JSONRPC_SERVER_ERROR_START
declarations.doc_comment: Start of the server-defined error code range (-32000).

declarations.name: JSONRPC_SERVER_ERROR_END
declarations.doc_comment: End of the server-defined error code range (-32099).

declarations.name: JsonrpcServerErrorCode
declarations.doc_comment: A server-defined error code in the -32000 to -32099 range.

declarations.name: JsonrpcErrorCode
declarations.doc_comment:
A valid JSON-RPC error code — one of the 5 standard codes or
a server-defined code in the -32000 to -32099 range.

declarations.name: JsonrpcErrorObject
declarations.doc_comment: Error object within a JSON-RPC error response.
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ code: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodLiteral<-32700>, ZodLiteral<-32600>, ZodLiteral<-32601>, ZodLiteral<-32602>, ZodLiteral<-32603>, $ZodBranded<...>]>; message: ZodString; data: ZodOptional<...>; }, $loose>

declarations.name: JsonrpcErrorResponse
declarations.doc_comment: A response that indicates an error occurred.
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ jsonrpc: ZodLiteral<"2.0">; id: ZodNullable<ZodUnion<readonly [ZodString, ZodNumber]>>; error: ZodObject<{ code: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodLiteral<-32700>, ... 4 more ..., $ZodBranded<...>]>; message: ZodString; data: ZodOptional<...>; }, $loose>; }, $loose>

declarations.name: JsonrpcResponseOrError
declarations.doc_comment: A successful response or an error response.
declarations.type_signature: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodObject<{ jsonrpc: ZodLiteral<"2.0">; id: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodString, ZodNumber]>; result: ZodJSONSchema; }, $loose>, ZodObject<...>]>

declarations.name: JsonrpcMessage
declarations.doc_comment: Any valid JSON-RPC message (request, notification, response, or error response).
declarations.type_signature: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodObject<{ jsonrpc: ZodLiteral<"2.0">; id: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodString, ZodNumber]>; method: ZodString; params: ZodOptional<ZodObject<{}, $loose>>; }, $loose>, ZodObject<...>, ZodObject<...>, ZodObject<...>]>

declarations.name: JsonrpcMessageFromClientToServer
declarations.doc_comment: Messages a client can send to a server (request or notification).
declarations.type_signature: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodObject<{ jsonrpc: ZodLiteral<"2.0">; id: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodString, ZodNumber]>; method: ZodString; params: ZodOptional<ZodObject<{}, $loose>>; }, $loose>, ZodObject<...>]>

declarations.name: JsonrpcMessageFromServerToClient
declarations.doc_comment: Messages a server can send to a client (notification, response, or error response).
declarations.type_signature: ZodUnion<readonly [ZodObject<{ jsonrpc: ZodLiteral<"2.0">; method: ZodString; params: ZodOptional<ZodObject<{}, $loose>>; }, $loose>, ZodObject<...>, ZodObject<...>]>

modules.module_comment:
JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope schemas for RPC dispatch and SAES transport.

Params are object-only following MCP (no positional arrays); per-action
spec.input narrows further. Result is the full JSON-RPC valid value
space per spec §5 ("determined by the method invoked"): object, array,
string, number, boolean, or null — the per-action spec.output is the
actual contract. The envelope-level loose-object-only constraint
inherited from MCP would reject any action with `output: z.null()` /
`z.string()` / etc. on the wire, so we relax it here while keeping
MCP-superset shape on params + _meta.

MCP `_meta` types (`JsonrpcMcpMeta`, `JsonrpcRequestParamsMeta`) are
exported as building blocks for per-action schemas but are NOT validated
at the envelope level — `_meta` content validation belongs in per-action
schemas where it produces the correct error code (`invalid_params`, not
`invalid_request`).

@source https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk
@see https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification

declarations.name: MiddlewareSpec
declarations.doc_comment: A named middleware layer.

properties.name: errors
properties.doc_comment: Error response schemas this middleware can produce, keyed by HTTP status code.

modules.module_comment:
Middleware spec type — named middleware layer definition.

Separated from `http/route_spec.ts` so middleware modules can import this
type without creating an upward dependency on routes.

declarations.name: parse_allowed_origins
declarations.doc_comment:
Parses FUZ_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var into regex matchers for request source verification.
Origin allowlisting for locally-running services — not the CSRF layer
(that's `SameSite: strict` on session cookies).

Accepts comma-separated patterns with limited wildcards:
- Exact origins: `https://api.fuz.dev`
- Wildcard subdomains: `https://*.fuz.dev` (matches exactly one subdomain level)
- Multiple wildcards: `https://*.staging.*.fuz.dev` (for deep subdomains)
- Wildcard ports: `http://localhost:*` (matches any port or no port)
- IPv6 addresses: `http://[::1]:3000`, `https://[2001:db8::1]`
- Combined: `https://*.fuz.dev:*`

Examples:
- `http://localhost:3000,https://prod.fuz.dev`
- `https://*.api.fuz.dev,http://127.0.0.1:*`
- `http://[::1]:*,https://*.*.corp.fuz.dev:*`

throws.description: if any individual pattern is invalid (missing protocol, partial wildcards, etc.)

declarations.name: should_allow_origin
declarations.doc_comment:
Tests if a request source (origin or referer) matches any of the allowed patterns.
Pattern matching is case-insensitive for domains (as per web standards).

declarations.name: verify_request_source
declarations.doc_comment:
Middleware that verifies the request source against an allowlist.

Origin allowlisting (not the CSRF layer — that's `SameSite: strict` cookies):
- Checks the `Origin` header (if present) against the allowlist
- Allows requests without an `Origin` header (direct access, curl, etc.)

Origin-only by design — Fetch spec mandates `Origin` on every unsafe
method (POST / PUT / DELETE / PATCH) regardless of `Referrer-Policy`,
so every real browser request on the state-changing surface carries it.
Non-browser clients (curl, server-to-server, CLI) don't ship auto-
attached session cookies, so CSRF isn't the relevant threat there —
auth (bearer / daemon token) is the actual control. A Referer fallback
would only widen the accepted-shape envelope without closing a real
CSRF hole; mirrors `zzz_server::auth::is_request_origin_allowed`.

modules.module_comment:
Request source verification middleware for API protection.

Verifies requests are coming from expected origins/referers.
CSRF protection is provided by `SameSite: strict` on session cookies
(see `auth/session_middleware.ts`). This module provides origin allowlisting
for locally-running services — preventing untrusted websites from
making requests as the user browses the web.

declarations.name: EmitAfterCommitContext
declarations.doc_comment:
Minimal structural context required by `emit_after_commit`. Both
`RouteContext` and `ActionContext` satisfy this — they each carry
`log` and `post_commit_effects`.

declarations.name: emit_after_commit
declarations.doc_comment:
Defer a side effect until after the handler's transaction commits.

Pushes a raw thunk onto `ctx.post_commit_effects` — the flush
middleware (in `server/app_server.ts` and the per-message WS dispatcher)
is the only site that ever invokes `fn`. This is load-bearing: a
previous implementation queued `Promise.resolve().then(fn)`, which
JS's microtask scheduler drains before the wrapping
`await db.query('COMMIT')` resumes — `fn` fired mid-transaction and a
rollback would leak a notification for state that never landed.

The thunk shape closes that gap by deferring the work to flush time.
The flush owns the per-thunk `try/catch` + `log.error` so any
directly-pushed thunk (tests included) cannot escape the safety net.

parameters.name: fn
parameters.description: side effect to run after commit; may return `void` or `Promise<void>`

declarations.name: flush_pending_effects
declarations.doc_comment:
Drain an eager `pending_effects` queue: `Promise.allSettled` the
in-flight handles, route every rejection through `log.error`, and
fan out to `on_rejection` when supplied (production wires this to
`on_effect_error` for monitoring).

Returned promise resolves once every effect has settled. Never
rejects. No-op when `effects` is empty (common on read-only
requests).

Symmetric with `flush_post_commit_effects` for the deferred queue.
declarations.type_signature: (effects: readonly Promise<void>[], log: Logger, on_rejection?: ((reason: unknown) => void) | undefined): Promise<void>

declarations.name: flush_post_commit_effects
declarations.doc_comment:
Drain a `post_commit_effects` queue: invoke each thunk under
`try/catch`, collect any returned promises, and `Promise.allSettled`
them. Synchronous throws and async rejections are routed through
`log.error` so one failing effect cannot starve siblings.

Returned promise resolves once every thunk has finished. Never
rejects.
declarations.type_signature: (effects: readonly (() => void | Promise<void>)[], log: Logger): Promise<void>

modules.module_comment:
Two-queue side-effect machinery for request handlers.

Handlers register fire-and-forget work in one of two queues, distinguished
by their timing contract:

- `pending_effects: Array<Promise<void>>` — eager. Producers push pool
  writes that are already in flight (audit emits, session-touch UPDATE,
  api-token usage tracking). The pool write is rollback-resilient by
  virtue of running outside the request transaction; pushing the
  in-flight handle lets test mode (`await_pending_effects: true`) await
  it.
- `post_commit_effects: Array<() => void | Promise<void>>` — deferred.
  Producers go through `emit_after_commit(ctx, fn)`; the flush
  middleware is the only site that ever invokes the thunk, and it does
  so after the request handler (and its wrapping `db.transaction`)
  returns. Used for WS sends and any work that must observe a committed
  transaction.

The split exists because the two shapes encode different contracts:
eager pushers are saying "wait for this work that's already started";
thunk pushers are saying "run this after the handler returns." Burying
both behind one `Array<PendingEffect>` made `c.var.pending_effects.push(x)`
ambiguous at the call site. With separate queues, the field name is
the contract.

Both `RouteContext` (HTTP routes) and `ActionContext` (RPC + WS
actions) carry both queues by convention, so this module stays in
`http/` (every transport depends on it).

declarations.name: normalize_ip
declarations.doc_comment:
Normalize an IP address for consistent matching and storage.

Delegates to `canonicalize_ip` from `ip_canonical.ts` — collapses
RFC 5952-equivalent IPv6 forms (`::1`, `::0001`, `0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1`)
into a single key, emits IPv4-mapped IPv6 in dotted form, and
strips the `::ffff:` prefix from dotted IPv4-mapped values so the
bucket collapses to plain IPv4.

- Lowercases for case-insensitive IPv6 comparison.
- Idempotent: calling twice produces the same result.
- Safe on non-IP strings: `normalize_ip('unknown')` returns `'unknown'`.
  Malformed inputs (`'attacker:controlled'`, `'::1\n'`,
  `'203.0.113.1:8080'`) pass through unchanged so downstream
  `validate_ip_strict` can still reject them — canonicalization
  never erases the malformed-form signal.

declarations.name: ProxyOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Configuration for trusted proxy resolution.

properties.name: trusted_proxies
properties.doc_comment: Trusted proxy IPs or CIDR ranges (e.g. `'127.0.0.1'`, `'10.0.0.0/8'`, `'::1'`).

properties.name: get_connection_ip
properties.doc_comment: Extract the raw TCP connection IP from the Hono context.

properties.name: log
properties.doc_comment: Optional logger for proxy resolution diagnostics.

declarations.name: ParsedProxy
declarations.doc_comment: A parsed proxy entry — either an exact IP or a CIDR range.

declarations.name: parse_proxy_entry
declarations.doc_comment:
Parse a trusted proxy entry string into a structured form.

Accepts plain IPs (`'127.0.0.1'`, `'::1'`) and CIDR notation (`'10.0.0.0/8'`, `'fe80::/10'`).
Plain IPs are normalized (lowercase, IPv4-mapped IPv6 stripped) and validated.
CIDR prefixes are validated against address family bounds.

throws.description: on invalid IP, invalid CIDR network, or NaN/negative/over-range prefix

declarations.name: validate_ip_strict
declarations.doc_comment:
Strict IP validity check.

Defense in depth around Hono's `hono/utils/ipaddr` helpers, which are
lax in two ways:

1. `distinctRemoteAddr` classifies anything-with-a-colon as `'IPv6'`,
   including `'host:port'`, `'attacker:controlled'`, `'203.0.113.1:8080'`.
2. `convertIPv6ToBinary` silently accepts malformed forms like
   `'[::1]:8080'` and `'::1\n'`, parsing them as inconsistent binary
   values that would still serve as distinct rate-limit keys for an
   attacker rotating the suffix.

Strict validation here is two-layered: a character-set pre-filter
(`IP_LITERAL_CHARS`), then a round-trip through `convertIPv*ToBinary`
to confirm the input parses cleanly. Either layer alone has holes;
together they reject every input form we've seen Hono mis-handle.

Used as the security primitive for any code path that takes an IP
string from an untrusted source (XFF, query params) and uses it as a
key (rate limiting, audit subject) or compares it against trusted
proxies via CIDR (where the latent throw would otherwise bubble out).
declarations.return_description:
the address family on success, `undefined` if the string is
not a strictly-valid IP

declarations.name: is_trusted_ip
declarations.doc_comment:
Check whether `ip` matches any entry in the trusted proxy list.

Normalizes `ip` before matching (lowercase, IPv4-mapped IPv6 stripped).
Uses `validate_ip_strict` to reject malformed input — without strict
validation, Hono's lax `distinctRemoteAddr` would let an entry like
`'203.0.113.1:8080'` (false-positive `'IPv6'`) reach
`convertIPv6ToBinary` in the CIDR-match branch and throw.

declarations.name: resolve_client_ip
declarations.doc_comment:
Resolve the real client IP from an `X-Forwarded-For` header value.

Walks right-to-left, skipping trusted proxy entries AND any entry
that fails strict IP validation (`validate_ip_strict`). The first
untrusted, strictly-valid entry is the client IP. If every walked
entry is trusted or malformed, returns the leftmost strictly-valid
(trusted) entry (likely-misconfigured all-trusted case) or
`undefined` (everything was malformed — middleware falls back to
the connection IP). All entries are normalized before matching and
in the returned value.

Skipping malformed entries is the rate-limit-key fix for the
"attacker controls XFF and the proxy passes it through" surface —
without the skip, an attacker could rotate arbitrary strings (incl.
`'attacker:controlled'`, which Hono's lax `distinctRemoteAddr`
misclassifies as IPv6) as XFF values to get fresh per-IP rate-limit
buckets. Tradeoff: legitimate non-standard proxies that include
ports in XFF entries (e.g. `203.0.113.1:8080`) also fail strict
validation, so those entries get skipped and the rate-limit bucket
collapses to the proxy's connection IP (one bucket for everyone
behind that proxy). Standard proxies (nginx, cloud LBs) don't
include ports.
declarations.return_description: the normalized client IP, or `undefined` if the header is empty / all entries malformed

declarations.name: create_proxy_middleware
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a Hono middleware that resolves the client IP from trusted proxies.

Sets `client_ip` on the Hono context for downstream use by `get_client_ip`.
All client IPs are normalized (lowercase, IPv4-mapped IPv6 stripped).

Resolution logic:
1. No `X-Forwarded-For` → use connection IP directly.
2. `X-Forwarded-For` present but connection is untrusted → ignore header
   (spoofed by a direct attacker), use connection IP.
3. `X-Forwarded-For` present and connection is trusted → walk header
   right-to-left, strip trusted entries, use first untrusted entry.

throws.description: if any entry in `options.trusted_proxies` is invalid (parsed eagerly via `parse_proxy_entry`)

declarations.name: create_proxy_middleware_spec
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a middleware spec for trusted proxy resolution.

Apply before auth middleware so `client_ip` is available for rate limiting.

declarations.name: get_client_ip
declarations.doc_comment:
Read the resolved client IP from the Hono context.

Returns `'unknown'` if the proxy middleware has not run or no IP is available.
Set by `create_proxy_middleware`.

modules.module_comment:
Trusted proxy configuration and middleware.

Resolves the client IP from `X-Forwarded-For` only when the TCP connection
originates from a configured trusted proxy. Without this middleware,
`get_client_ip` returns `'unknown'`.

declarations.name: get_route_input
declarations.doc_comment:
Get validated input from the Hono context.

Call after the input validation middleware has run. Pass the route's
`input` Zod schema to infer the typed shape directly:

```ts
const input = get_route_input(c, my_route_spec.input);
```

Or pass an explicit type argument when the schema isn't in scope:

```ts
const input = get_route_input<MyInput>(c);
```
declarations.type_signature: <S extends z.ZodType>(c: Context<any, any, {}>, schema: S): output<S>

declarations.name: get_route_params
declarations.doc_comment:
Get validated URL path params from the Hono context.

Call after the params validation middleware has run. Pass the route's
`params` schema to infer the typed shape, or supply an explicit type
argument. See `get_route_input` for the two call shapes.
declarations.type_signature: <S extends z.ZodType>(c: Context<any, any, {}>, schema: S): output<S>

declarations.name: get_route_query
declarations.doc_comment:
Get validated URL query params from the Hono context.

Call after the query validation middleware has run. Pass the route's
`query` schema to infer the typed shape, or supply an explicit type
argument. See `get_route_input` for the two call shapes.
declarations.type_signature: <S extends z.ZodType>(c: Context<any, any, {}>, schema: S): output<S>

declarations.name: AuthGuards
declarations.doc_comment:
Two-phase auth guard set returned by `AuthGuardResolver`.

`pre_validation` runs before input validation — 401 checks live here
so unauthenticated callers never see route-shape information from
input parsing failures. `post_authorization` runs after the
authorization phase has populated `RequestContext` — role / keeper
checks live here because they read `c.var.request_context.role_grants`.

declarations.name: AuthGuardResolver
declarations.doc_comment:
Resolves a `RouteAuth` to middleware guard handlers.

Injected into `apply_route_specs` to decouple route registration
from auth-specific middleware. See `fuz_auth_guard_resolver` in
`auth/auth_guard_resolver.ts` for the standard implementation.

declarations.name: AuthorizationHandler
declarations.doc_comment:
Per-route authorization phase. Runs after pre-validation auth guards
AND input validation; resolves the acting actor (when `auth.actor !== 'none'`)
by reading `c.var.validated_input.acting` and sets the request context on
the Hono context. Per-route order in `apply_route_specs`: params → query
→ pre-validation auth (401) → input validation (400) → authorization
phase → post-authorization auth (403) → handler.

Returns a `Response` to short-circuit (resolution failure → 400 / 500),
or `void` to continue. The http framework stays auth-agnostic — fuz_app
provides the implementation via `create_fuz_authorization_handler` in
`auth/request_context.ts`.

declarations.name: RouteMethod
declarations.doc_comment: HTTP methods supported by route specs.

declarations.name: RouteContext
declarations.doc_comment:
Per-request deps provided by the framework to route handlers.

Audit writes and other rollback-resilient fire-and-forget calls run
through `AppDeps.audit.emit(ctx, input)` (see `auth/audit_emitter.ts`),
which captures the pool inside its closure — handlers can never
accidentally write audits against the request transaction.

Routes whose body manages its own transaction (signup, bootstrap)
declare `transaction: false` on the spec, which makes `route.db` the
pool — they reach for it directly.

properties.name: db
properties.doc_comment:
Transaction-scoped when `RouteSpec.transaction` is true (the default
for non-GET); pool-level otherwise.

properties.name: pending_effects
properties.doc_comment:
Eager fire-and-forget queue — push the in-flight `Promise<void>` for
pool writes already running (audit emits, session touch, api-token
usage tracking). The flush middleware drains via
`flush_pending_effects` after the handler returns.

properties.name: post_commit_effects
properties.doc_comment:
Deferred post-commit thunks — do not push directly; reach for
`emit_after_commit(ctx, fn)` from `pending_effects.ts`. The flush
middleware invokes each thunk after the handler (and any wrapping
`db.transaction`) returns, closing the microtask-ordering window
that an eager `Promise.resolve().then(fn)` leaves open inside the
transaction.

declarations.name: RouteHandler
declarations.doc_comment:
Route handler function — receives the Hono context and a `RouteContext`
with per-request deps (db, pending_effects).

TypeScript allows fewer params, so handlers that don't need `route`
can use `(c) => ...` without changes.

declarations.name: RouteSpec
declarations.doc_comment:
A single route definition — the unit of the surface map.

`input` and `output` schemas align with SAES `ActionSpec` naming.
Use `z.null()` for routes with no request body (GET, DELETE without body).

properties.name: params
properties.doc_comment:
URL path parameter schema. Use `z.strictObject()` with string fields matching `:param` segments.

REST-only — actions dispatch through a single JSON-RPC endpoint and encode
everything in `input`, so `params` doesn't appear on `ActionSpec`.

properties.name: query
properties.doc_comment: URL query parameter schema. Use `z.strictObject()` with string fields.

properties.name: input
properties.doc_comment: Request body schema. Use `z.null()` for routes with no body.

properties.name: output
properties.doc_comment: Success response body schema.

properties.name: rate_limit
properties.doc_comment:
Rate limit key type — declares what this route's rate limiter is keyed on.

When set, 429 (`RateLimitError`) is auto-derived in `derive_error_schemas`.
The actual `RateLimiter` instance is still wired imperatively in the handler —
this field is metadata for surface introspection and policy invariants.

properties.name: errors
properties.doc_comment:
Handler-specific error response schemas keyed by HTTP status code.

Middleware errors (auth 401/403, validation 400, rate limit 429) are
auto-derived from `auth`, `input`, and `rate_limit`. Declare handler-specific
errors here (e.g., 404 for not-found, 409 for conflicts).

Explicit entries override auto-derived ones for the same status code.

properties.name: transaction
properties.doc_comment:
Whether to wrap the handler in a database transaction.

When omitted, defaults are derived from the HTTP method:
- `GET` → `false` (read-only, no transaction)
- All others (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) → `true`

Set explicitly to override the default (e.g., `false` for a POST
that manages its own transaction like signup).

declarations.name: apply_middleware_specs
declarations.doc_comment: Apply named middleware specs to a Hono app.

declarations.name: apply_route_specs
declarations.doc_comment:
Apply route specs to a Hono app.

For each spec: resolves auth to guards via the provided resolver,
adds input validation middleware (for routes with non-null input schemas),
runs the optional authorization phase to resolve the acting actor + build
the request context, wraps handler with DEV-only output and error
validation, wraps with error catch layer (catches `ThrownJsonrpcError`
and generic errors), and registers the route.

Per-route middleware order: params → query → pre-validation auth
guards (401) → input validation (400) → authorization phase →
post-authorization auth guards (403) → handler. The 401 check runs
before any body parsing so unauthenticated callers never see
route-shape information from parse failures. Input validation runs
before the authorization phase (validate first, authorize after) so
the authorization phase reads `c.var.validated_input.acting` as a
typed Zod field instead of pre-parsing the raw body. Role /
credential-type denials still surface 403 last; trade-off is that
authenticated-but-unauthorized callers can distinguish 400
(validation) from 403 (authorization), a defense-in-depth concession
deemed acceptable because the route surface is public via
spec/codegen JSON.

Each handler receives a `RouteContext` with:
- `db`: transaction-scoped when `RouteSpec.transaction` is true; pool-level otherwise
- `pending_effects`: eager fire-and-forget pool-write queue
- `post_commit_effects`: deferred-thunk queue (push via `emit_after_commit`)

Also enforces registry-time invariant 2 from the auth-shape design:
`auth.actor !== 'none' ⟺ input or query declares acting?: ActingActor`.
REST is bi-located (GETs declare `acting` on `query`, mutations on
`input`), so the check passes both slots; the action-dispatcher
registries (`compile_action_registry`) share the same helper with
`input` only — `ActionSpec` has no `query` shape.

throws.description: if two specs share the same `method` + `path` (each combination must be unique), or if any spec violates the actor-acting biconditional

declarations.type_signature: (app: Hono<BlankEnv, BlankSchema, "/">, specs: RouteSpec[], resolve_auth_guards: AuthGuardResolver, log: Logger, db: Db, authorize?: AuthorizationHandler | undefined): void

parameters.name: authorize
parameters.description: optional authorization phase; runs after input validation

declarations.name: prefix_route_specs
declarations.doc_comment: Prepend a prefix to all route spec paths.
declarations.return_description: a new array — the input specs are not mutated

modules.module_comment:
Introspectable route spec system for Hono apps.

Routes are defined as data (method, path, auth, input/output schemas, handler),
then applied to Hono. The attack surface is generated from the specs —
always accurate, always complete.

Input/output schemas align with SAES `ActionSpec` conventions:
- `input`: Zod schema for the request body (`z.null()` for no body)
- `output`: Zod schema for the success response body
- `z.strictObject()` for inputs (reject unknown keys)

declarations.name: is_null_schema
declarations.doc_comment:
Check if a schema is exactly `z.null()`.

Uses `instanceof` rather than runtime parsing to avoid false positives
from `z.nullable(z.string())` or similar schemas that accept null
but also accept other values.

declarations.name: is_void_schema
declarations.doc_comment:
Check if a schema is exactly `z.void()`.

RPC action specs use `z.void()` to declare a parameterless method —
JSON-RPC 2.0 forbids `params: null` (params must be omitted or be a
Structured value), so `z.void()` is the correct schema for "no params"
and the dispatcher maps absent params to `undefined` for these specs.

declarations.name: is_strict_object_schema
declarations.doc_comment:
Check if a schema is a strict object (`z.strictObject()`).

Strict objects set `catchall` to `ZodNever` to reject unknown keys.
Regular `z.object()` has `catchall: undefined` (strips unknown keys in Zod 4).

declarations.name: schema_to_surface
declarations.doc_comment:
Convert a Zod schema to a JSON-serializable representation for the surface.

Returns `null` for null schemas, JSON Schema for object schemas.

declarations.name: middleware_applies
declarations.doc_comment:
Check if a middleware path pattern applies to a route path.

Supports Hono-style patterns:
- `/api/*` matches `/api/anything`
- `/api/zap/*` matches `/api/zap/runs` but not `/api/account/login`
- Exact match: `/health` matches `/health`

declarations.name: merge_error_schemas
declarations.doc_comment:
Merge auto-derived, middleware, and explicit error schemas for a route spec.

Merge order: derived -> middleware -> explicit route errors.
Later layers override earlier ones for the same status code.
declarations.type_signature: (spec: { auth: { account: "none" | "optional" | "required"; actor: "none" | "optional" | "required"; roles?: readonly string[] | undefined; credential_types?: readonly string[] | undefined; }; input: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<...>>; params?: ZodObject<...> | undefined; query?: ZodObject<...> | undefined; rate_limit?: "both" | ... 2 more ... | undefined; errors?: Partial<...> | undefined; }, middleware_errors?: Partial<...> | ... 1 more ... | undefined): Partial<...> | null
declarations.return_description: merged error schemas, or `null` if empty

parameters.name: spec
parameters.type: { auth: { account: "none" | "optional" | "required"; actor: "none" | "optional" | "required"; roles?: readonly string[] | undefined; credential_types?: readonly string[] | undefined; }; input: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>; params?: ZodObject<...> | undefined; query?: ZodObject<...> ...

parameters.name: middleware_errors
parameters.type: Partial<Record<number, ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>>> | null | undefined
parameters.description: errors contributed by middleware whose path matches the route

modules.module_comment:
Shared pure helpers for schema introspection and middleware matching.

Used by both `http/route_spec.ts` (input validation) and `http/surface.ts`
(attack surface generation). Extracted to avoid circular dependencies
between routes and middleware.

declarations.name: filter_protected_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that require any form of authentication.

declarations.name: filter_public_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that are publicly accessible (no auth surface at all).

declarations.name: filter_role_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter all role-guarded routes (any role declared on `auth.roles`).

declarations.name: filter_authenticated_routes
declarations.doc_comment:
Filter routes that require basic authentication only — `account === 'required'`
with no role / credential gate.

declarations.name: filter_keeper_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that require keeper credentials (`daemon_token`).

declarations.name: filter_routes_for_role
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes whose `auth.roles` includes the named role.

declarations.name: RouteAuthCategory
declarations.doc_comment:
Categorize a `RouteAuth` into one of the legacy auth buckets.

Returns:
- `'none'` for fully public routes (account === 'none' && actor === 'none')
- `'keeper'` when `credential_types` includes `'daemon_token'`
- `'role:<name>'` for each role declared on `auth.roles` (multi-role specs
  are emitted multiple times; callers that need single-bucket grouping
  should pre-collapse)
- `'authenticated'` for `account === 'required'` without role / credential gate
- `'optional'` when either axis is `'optional'` and no other bucket fits
- `'other'` as a last-resort bucket for shapes that don't match above

declarations.name: routes_by_auth_type
declarations.doc_comment:
Group routes by auth category (see `RouteAuthCategory`). Multi-role specs
appear under each of their role buckets.

declarations.name: filter_routes_by_prefix
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes whose path starts with `prefix`.

declarations.name: filter_routes_with_input
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that have a non-null input schema.

declarations.name: filter_routes_with_params
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that have a non-null params schema.

declarations.name: filter_routes_with_query
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that have a non-null query schema.

declarations.name: filter_mutation_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that are mutations (POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH).

declarations.name: filter_rate_limited_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that declare rate limiting.

declarations.name: format_route_key
declarations.doc_comment: Format a route as `'METHOD /path'` (e.g. `'GET /health'`).

declarations.name: surface_auth_summary
declarations.doc_comment:
Summarize route auth distribution across the surface.

Categorical view over the four-axis flat record. Multi-role specs
contribute one count per role they admit.
declarations.type_signature: (surface: AppSurface): { none: number; authenticated: number; optional: number; role: Map<string, number>; keeper: number; other: number; }
declarations.return_type: { none: number; authenticated: number; optional: number; role: Map<string, number>; keeper: number; other: number; }
declarations.return_description: counts by auth category, with role counts broken out by role name

modules.module_comment:
Pure query functions over `AppSurface` data.

Usable in tests, the adversarial auth runner, and future surface explorer UI.
Replaces duplicated inline `.filter()` patterns.

Categorical filters (`filter_authenticated_routes`, `filter_role_routes`,
`filter_keeper_routes`) group the new flat-record `RouteAuth` shape into
the legacy categorical buckets (`'authenticated'`, `'role'`, `'keeper'`)
for adversarial test runners and the surface explorer. The buckets are
derived views over the four axes (`account`, `actor`, `roles`,
`credential_types`) — see `http/auth_shape.ts` for the canonical shape.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceRoute
declarations.doc_comment: A route in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: is_mutation
properties.doc_comment: Whether this route mutates state (POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH).

properties.name: transaction
properties.doc_comment: Whether this route's handler runs inside a database transaction.

properties.name: rate_limit_key
properties.doc_comment: Rate limit key type declared on the route spec. `null` when not rate-limited.

properties.name: params_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the URL path params schema. `null` when no params.

properties.name: query_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the URL query params schema. `null` when no query schema.

properties.name: input_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the request body schema. `null` for no-body routes.

properties.name: output_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the success response schema.

properties.name: error_schemas
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representations of error responses, keyed by HTTP status code. `null` when none.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceMiddleware
declarations.doc_comment: A middleware in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: error_schemas
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representations of error responses, keyed by HTTP status code. `null` when none.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceEnv
declarations.doc_comment: An env var in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: sensitivity
properties.doc_comment: Sensitivity level from `.meta({sensitivity})`. `null` when not sensitive.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceEvent
declarations.doc_comment: An SSE event in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

declarations.name: AppSurfaceRpcMethod
declarations.doc_comment: A method within an RPC endpoint in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: input_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the input schema. `null` for null-input methods.

properties.name: output_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the output schema.

properties.name: rate_limit_key
properties.doc_comment: Rate limit key declared on the action spec. `null` when not rate-limited.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceRpcEndpoint
declarations.doc_comment: An RPC endpoint in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

declarations.name: AppSurfaceWsMethod
declarations.doc_comment: A method within a WebSocket endpoint in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: kind
properties.doc_comment: `request_response` (inbound dispatch) or `remote_notification` (server → client).

properties.name: auth
properties.doc_comment:
Per-action auth shape. `null` for `remote_notification` (server →
client) — notifications have no inbound dispatch and therefore no
auth axis. `request_response` always carries a `RouteAuth`.

properties.name: input_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema of the input schema. `null` for nullary inputs.

properties.name: output_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema of the output schema.

properties.name: rate_limit_key
properties.doc_comment: Rate limit key declared on the action spec. `null` when not rate-limited.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceWsEndpoint
declarations.doc_comment: A WebSocket endpoint in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: allowed_origins
properties.doc_comment:
Upgrade-time origin allowlist, one entry per `WsEndpointSpec.allowed_origins`
regex stringified via `RegExp.prototype.toString()` (`'/<source>/<flags>'`).
Empty array when no origins were declared (any-origin); reviewers read this
as the exact pattern matched at the upgrade gate, not a wildcard
approximation. Reconstruct via `new RegExp(source, flags)` if needed.

properties.name: required_roles
properties.doc_comment:
Upgrade-time role gate — empty array when no `required_roles` was
declared (any-authenticated). Documents the coarse gate; per-action
`auth` on each method covers per-message authorization.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Assembly-time diagnostic collected during surface generation or server assembly.

declarations.name: AppSurface
declarations.doc_comment: Generated attack surface — JSON-serializable.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceSpec
declarations.doc_comment:
The surface bundled with the source specs that produced it.

`AppSurface` is JSON-serializable (snapshots, UI, startup logging) —
it's the observability layer, written to disk by `gro gen` for human
inspection + drift detection.

`AppSurfaceSpec` is runtime-only — tests, introspection, attack
surface assertions. Both in-process and cross-process tests construct
an `AppSurfaceSpec` in TS via `create_test_app_surface_spec` (or a
consumer equivalent); the cross-process-ness lives in the transport
+ per-test fixture, not the schema source.

declarations.name: RpcEndpointSpec
declarations.doc_comment: An RPC endpoint definition for surface generation.

declarations.name: GenerateAppSurfaceOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `generate_app_surface`.

properties.name: ws_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
Mounted WS endpoints (the same array `create_app_server.ws_endpoints`
auto-mounts). Each entry's actions surface into
`AppSurface.ws_endpoints[i].methods` for attack-surface tests +
startup logging.

declarations.name: collect_middleware_errors
declarations.doc_comment: Collect error schemas from all middleware that applies to a route path.
declarations.type_signature: (middleware: MiddlewareSpec[], route_path: string): Partial<Record<number, ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>>> | null
declarations.return_description: merged middleware error schemas, or `null` if none

declarations.name: env_schema_to_surface
declarations.doc_comment: Convert env schema to surface entries using `.meta()` metadata.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Zod object schema with `.meta()` on fields

declarations.name: events_to_surface
declarations.doc_comment: Convert SSE event specs to surface entries.

declarations.name: generate_app_surface
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate a JSON-serializable attack surface from middleware, route specs,
and optional env/event metadata.

declarations.name: create_app_surface_spec
declarations.doc_comment: Create an `AppSurfaceSpec` — the surface bundled with its source specs.

modules.module_comment:
App surface generation — JSON-serializable attack surface from route and middleware specs.

Pure schema helpers (`is_null_schema`, `schema_to_surface`, `middleware_applies`,
`merge_error_schemas`) live in `http/schema_helpers.ts`.

declarations.name: USERNAME_LENGTH_MIN
declarations.doc_comment: Minimum username length (must have start + middle + end characters).

declarations.name: USERNAME_LENGTH_MAX
declarations.doc_comment: Maximum username length (matches GitHub's limit).

declarations.name: USERNAME_PROVIDED_LENGTH_MAX
declarations.doc_comment: Maximum length for username input on login/lookup — more permissive than `USERNAME_LENGTH_MAX` for forward-compatibility if the creation limit is raised.

declarations.name: Username
declarations.doc_comment:
Username for account creation — starts with letter, alphanumeric/dash/underscore middle, ends with alphanumeric. No @ or . allowed.

Canonicalized to lowercase at parse time. The regex rejects whitespace
outright, so `.trim()` is unnecessary here. Storage is canonical across
every creation site (bootstrap, signup, admin-create, invite acceptance)
because the schema is the single source of truth — eliminates the
per-caller `trim().toLowerCase()` ritual and keeps the
`LOWER(username) = LOWER($1)` lookup contract simple.

declarations.name: UsernameProvided
declarations.doc_comment:
Username submitted for login or lookup — minimal validation for forward-compatibility if format rules change.

Canonicalized via `.trim().toLowerCase()` at parse time so login's
per-account rate-limit key and DB lookup see a uniform value
regardless of casing or surrounding whitespace. Mirrors the storage
canonicalization on `Username` so submission and storage agree.

declarations.name: Email
declarations.doc_comment:
Email validation. Lives here rather than `@fuzdev/fuz_util` because every
current consumer pairs it with `Username` (signup, invites, audit log) —
keeping the two together avoids a cross-package import for the
identity-primitive bundle. Promote to fuz_util if a non-identity consumer
surfaces.

modules.module_comment:
Reusable validator-schema primitives — `Username`, `UsernameProvided`,
`Email`. Lives at the top level (not inside `auth/` or `http/`) because
these shapes don't depend on or imply any domain — accounts hold a
username and email, but invites, password resets, future cell-sharing,
and other surfaces all reach for the same primitives without going
through auth.

Split out from `auth/account_schema.ts` so the auth module shrinks to
entity types + client-safe JSON shapes (its real responsibility) and
non-auth consumers can import these primitives without dragging the
auth domain along. Future cross-domain primitives (phone, url, slug)
land here too.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_RATE_LIMITER_MAX_KEYS
declarations.doc_comment:
Default tracked-key cap: bounds worst-case memory under key-enumeration
attacks (an attacker rotating source IPs cannot grow the backing map
indefinitely between cleanup ticks). Tuned to comfortably fit real
traffic for a single-instance deployment while capping memory at a few
MB in the worst case.

declarations.name: RateLimiterOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Configuration for a rate limiter instance.

properties.name: max_attempts
properties.doc_comment: Maximum allowed attempts within the window.

properties.name: window_ms
properties.doc_comment: Sliding window duration in milliseconds.

properties.name: cleanup_interval_ms
properties.doc_comment: Interval for pruning stale entries (0 disables the timer).

properties.name: max_keys
properties.doc_comment:
Maximum tracked keys. When exceeded, the least-recently-used key is
evicted — bounds memory under key-enumeration attacks. Default:
`DEFAULT_RATE_LIMITER_MAX_KEYS` (100_000). Pass `null` to disable the
cap (falls back to an unbounded `Map` — only recommended when the key
set is known to be closed, e.g. a per-account limiter keyed to a
bounded-size account table).

LRU trade-off: every `check` / `record` call marks the key as
most-recently-used, so keys under active attack stay fresh and won't
be evicted. A slow-burn attacker spread across many low-volume keys
can, however, drop out of the table and reset their budget — set
`max_keys` high enough to fit the expected legitimate key set and
this stays theoretical.

declarations.name: default_login_ip_rate_limit
declarations.doc_comment: Default options for per-IP login rate limiting: 5 attempts per 15 minutes.

declarations.name: default_login_account_rate_limit
declarations.doc_comment: Default options for per-account login rate limiting: 10 attempts per 30 minutes.

declarations.name: default_action_ip_rate_limit
declarations.doc_comment:
Default options for per-IP action-dispatcher rate limiting: 600 attempts
per 15 minutes. Shared by the HTTP RPC and WebSocket action dispatchers
(one budget per action, not per transport). Permissive — catches runaway
scripts and egregious oracle probes, but well above human or normal
automation pace. Tighten downstream for stricter deployments.

declarations.name: default_action_account_rate_limit
declarations.doc_comment:
Default options for per-actor action-dispatcher rate limiting: 1200
attempts per 15 minutes. Shared by the HTTP RPC and WebSocket action
dispatchers. Permissive — sustained ~80/min is well above any human
admin workflow; an oracle probing 10k addresses still finishes in
~2 hours, slow enough to surface in audit. Tighten downstream.

declarations.name: RateLimitResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result of a rate limit check or record operation.

properties.name: allowed
properties.doc_comment: Whether the request is allowed.

properties.name: remaining
properties.doc_comment: Remaining attempts before blocking.

properties.name: retry_after
properties.doc_comment: Seconds until the oldest active attempt expires (0 if allowed).

declarations.name: RateLimiter
declarations.doc_comment:
In-memory sliding window rate limiter.

Stores an array of timestamps per key. On `check`/`record`, timestamps
outside the window are pruned. `retry_after` reports seconds until the
oldest active timestamp expires.

The backing store is an `LruMap` when `options.max_keys` is a positive
number (default `DEFAULT_RATE_LIMITER_MAX_KEYS`) and a plain `Map` when
`max_keys` is `null`. The `LruMap` path bounds memory under
key-enumeration attack at the cost of a slight per-op overhead and the
LRU trade-off described on `RateLimiterOptions.max_keys`.

Parameters that accept `RateLimiter | null` (e.g. `ip_rate_limiter`,
`login_account_rate_limiter`) silently disable rate limiting when `null`
is passed — no checks are performed and all requests are allowed through.

members.name: check
members.doc_comment:
Check whether `key` is allowed without recording an attempt.

Prunes timestamps that fell outside the window as a side effect (and
removes the key entirely when none remain), so the backing map stays
bounded even under read-only traffic.

members.name: record
members.doc_comment: Record a failed attempt for `key` and return the updated result.

members.name: reset
members.doc_comment: Clear all attempts for `key` (e.g. after successful login).

members.name: cleanup
members.doc_comment: Remove entries whose timestamps are all outside the window.

members.name: dispose
members.doc_comment: Stop the cleanup timer. Safe to call multiple times.

declarations.name: create_rate_limiter
declarations.doc_comment: Create a `RateLimiter` with sensible defaults for per-IP login protection.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: override individual options; unset fields use `default_login_ip_rate_limit`

declarations.name: rate_limit_exceeded_response
declarations.doc_comment: Build a 429 rate-limit-exceeded JSON response with `Retry-After` header.
declarations.return_description: a 429 Response

parameters.name: retry_after
parameters.description: seconds until the client should retry

modules.module_comment:
In-memory sliding window rate limiter.

Tracks failed attempts per key (typically IP address) using a sliding
time window. No external dependencies — state resets on server restart.

declarations.name: AUDIT_LOG_CHANNEL
declarations.doc_comment: SSE channel the audit-log stream route publishes on.

declarations.name: disconnect_event_types
declarations.doc_comment:
Audit event types that trigger SSE stream disconnection.

`role_grant_revoke` requires the revoked role to match the guard's `required_role`
(or is skipped entirely when `required_role` is `null` — useful for streams
not gated by any specific role_grant).
`session_revoke_all` and `password_change` close every stream for the target account.
`session_revoke` closes only the stream tied to the specific revoked session
(matched by the blake3 session hash in `event.metadata.session_id`) — closing
all of a user's streams for a single-session revoke would be over-aggressive.

declarations.name: create_sse_auth_guard
declarations.doc_comment:
Create an audit event handler that closes SSE streams on auth changes.

Closes streams when:
- `role_grant_revoke` fires for the `required_role` targeting a connected subscriber
- `session_revoke_all` targets a connected subscriber (consistent invalidation)
- `password_change` targets a connected subscriber (sessions revoked implicitly)

The registry must use `account_id` as the identity key when subscribing
(passed as the third argument to `registry.subscribe()`).
declarations.type_signature: <T>(registry: SubscriberRegistry<T>, required_role: string | null, log: Logger): (event: AuditLogEvent) => void
declarations.return_description: an `on_audit_event` callback

parameters.name: required_role
parameters.description:
the role that grants access to the SSE endpoint,
or `null` to skip `role_grant_revoke` handling entirely (for streams not gated
by a specific role_grant)

declarations.name: AuditLogSse
declarations.doc_comment:
Convenience factory result for audit log SSE.

Satisfies `AuditLogRouteOptions['stream']` and provides the combined
`on_audit_event` callback (broadcast + guard).

properties.name: subscribe
properties.doc_comment: Subscribe function — pass as part of `stream` option to `create_audit_log_route_specs`.

properties.name: log
properties.doc_comment: Logger — pass as part of `stream` option to `create_audit_log_route_specs`.

properties.name: on_audit_event
properties.doc_comment: Combined broadcast + guard callback. Wired by `create_app_server`'s `audit_log_sse` option, or compose inside the consumer's `audit_factory` body.

properties.name: registry
properties.doc_comment: The underlying registry — exposed for subscriber count monitoring.

declarations.name: audit_log_event_specs
declarations.doc_comment:
SSE event specs for audit log events.

One spec per `AUDIT_EVENT_TYPES` entry, all sharing the `AuditLogEventJson` params schema.
Pass to `create_app_server`'s `event_specs` for surface generation and DEV validation.

declarations.name: AUDIT_LOG_SSE_MAX_PER_SCOPE
declarations.doc_comment:
Default max concurrent SSE subscribers per session scope for the audit log.

The audit log SSE subscribes with `scope = session_hash` and
`groups = [account_id]`. Only `scope` is capped — so this limits tabs
per session. An account's total streams across all sessions is bounded
transitively by `max_sessions × AUDIT_LOG_SSE_MAX_PER_SCOPE`. 10 tabs
per session is a comfortable ceiling for normal use; consumers raising
it above ~50 should consider server-side connection limits.

declarations.name: create_audit_log_sse
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a complete audit log SSE setup with broadcasting and auth guard.

Combines `SubscriberRegistry`, `create_sse_auth_guard`, and the broadcast
call into a single object. The result satisfies `AuditLogRouteOptions['stream']`
and provides the `on_audit_event` listener for the audit emitter's chain.

Most consumers pass `audit_log_sse: true` to `create_app_server` and never
touch this directly — the factory builds an `AuditLogSse`, appends
`audit_sse.on_audit_event` to `backend.deps.audit.on_event_chain`, and
exposes it via `AppServerContext.audit_sse`. Reach for the manual path
(compose inside `audit_factory` body, or
`audit.on_event_chain.push(audit_sse.on_audit_event)` post-assembly) only
when wiring outside `create_app_server`.
declarations.type_signature: (options: { role?: string | undefined; log: Logger; max_per_scope?: number | null | undefined; }): AuditLogSse
declarations.return_description: audit log SSE setup (stream options + `on_audit_event` + registry)

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { role?: string | undefined; log: Logger; max_per_scope?: number | null | undefined; }

modules.module_comment:
SSE auth guard and convenience factory for audit log SSE.

`create_sse_auth_guard` bridges audit events to `SubscriberRegistry.close_by_identity()`,
closing SSE streams when a subscriber's access is revoked (role revocation or
session invalidation).

`create_audit_log_sse` is a convenience factory that combines the registry,
guard, and broadcaster — making the secure path the easy path for consumers.

declarations.name: SseStream
declarations.doc_comment:
Generic SSE stream controller interface.

Transport-agnostic — works with any serializable type.

properties.name: send
properties.doc_comment: Send data to the client as a JSON SSE event.

properties.name: comment
properties.doc_comment: Send a comment (for keep-alive pings).

properties.name: close
properties.doc_comment: Close the stream.

properties.name: on_close
properties.doc_comment: Register a listener called when the stream closes (client disconnect or explicit close).

declarations.name: SseNotification
declarations.doc_comment:
Notification shape aligned with JSON-RPC 2.0.

Uses `{method, params}` to match the JSON-RPC notification format.

properties.name: method
properties.doc_comment: Notification method name (e.g. 'run_created', 'host_updated').

properties.name: params
properties.doc_comment: Method-specific payload.

declarations.name: create_sse_response
declarations.doc_comment:
Create an SSE response for a Hono context.

Wraps Hono's `streamSSE` to provide a `{response, stream}` API
compatible with `SubscriberRegistry` push-based broadcasting.
The callback suspends via a promise that resolves on client disconnect
or explicit `close()`, keeping the stream alive for external sends.

Uses `hono_stream.write()` directly (not `writeSSE`) to avoid
Hono's HTML callback resolution — keeps the same `data: JSON\n\n` format.
declarations.type_signature: <T = unknown>(c: Context<any, any, {}>, log: Logger): { response: Response; stream: SseStream<T>; }
declarations.return_description: object with the streaming `Response` and an `SseStream` controller

parameters.name: log
parameters.description: logger for serialization and `on_close` listener errors

declarations.name: SSE_CONNECTED_COMMENT
declarations.doc_comment: SSE comment sent on connect to flush headers through proxies. Exported for test assertions.

declarations.name: EventSpec
declarations.doc_comment: Spec for a push event — declares params schema, description, and channel.

properties.name: method
properties.doc_comment: Event method name, used as the JSON-RPC notification `method`.

properties.name: params
properties.doc_comment: Zod schema for the notification `params` payload.

properties.name: description
properties.doc_comment: Human-readable description for surface output and docs.

properties.name: channel
properties.doc_comment: Channel this event broadcasts on. Omit for cross-channel events.

declarations.name: create_validated_broadcaster
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a broadcaster that validates events in DEV mode.

In DEV: warns on unknown methods and invalid params.
In production: passes through with zero overhead.
declarations.type_signature: <T extends SseNotification>(broadcaster: { broadcast: (channel: string, data: T) => void; }, event_specs: EventSpec[], log: Logger): { broadcast: (channel: string, data: T) => void; }
declarations.return_description: validated broadcaster wrapper (passthrough in production)

parameters.name: log
parameters.description: logger used to emit DEV warnings on unknown methods or param mismatches

modules.module_comment:
SSE (Server-Sent Events) streaming utilities for Hono.

Provides generic helpers for creating SSE response streams
and a notification type aligned with JSON-RPC 2.0.

properties.name: channels
properties.doc_comment: Channels this subscriber listens to. `null` means all channels.

properties.name: scope
properties.doc_comment: Primary (capped) identity. `null` when none.

properties.name: groups
properties.doc_comment: Grouping identities for `close_by_identity`. `null` when none.

declarations.name: SubscriberRegistryOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `SubscriberRegistry`.

properties.name: max_per_scope
properties.doc_comment:
Max subscribers sharing a single `scope`. On subscribe, when the count
of subscribers with the same `scope` reaches this limit, the oldest
matching subscriber(s) are closed before the new one is added.
`null` (default) disables the cap. `groups` identities are never capped.

declarations.name: SubscribeOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `SubscriberRegistry.subscribe`.

properties.name: channels
properties.doc_comment: Channels to subscribe to. Empty/absent = all channels.

properties.name: scope
properties.doc_comment:
Primary (capped) identity — e.g., session hash. Subject to
`max_per_scope` and matched by `close_by_identity`.

properties.name: groups
properties.doc_comment:
Grouping identities — e.g., account id. Matched by `close_by_identity`
but NOT subject to the cap. Use for coarse-targeted close.

declarations.name: SubscriberRegistry
declarations.doc_comment:
Generic subscriber registry with channel-based filtering and identity-keyed disconnection.

Subscribers connect with optional channel filters, a capped `scope`, and
uncapped `groups`. Broadcasts go to a specific channel and reach only
matching subscribers. `close_by_identity` force-closes all subscribers
whose `scope` or `groups` contain the given key — use for auth revocation.

members.name: subscribe
members.doc_comment: Add a subscriber.
members.return_description: unsubscribe function

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: SSE stream to send data to

members.name: broadcast
members.doc_comment:
Broadcast data to all subscribers on a channel.

Subscribers with no channel filter receive all broadcasts.
Subscribers with a channel filter only receive matching broadcasts.

members.name: close_by_identity
members.doc_comment:
Force-close all subscribers whose `scope` or `groups` match the given key.

Closes each matching stream and removes the subscriber from the registry.
Use for auth revocation — when a user's permissions change, close their
SSE connections so they must reconnect and re-authenticate.
members.return_description: the number of subscribers closed

parameters.name: identity
parameters.description: the identity key to match (checked against `scope` and `groups`)

modules.module_comment:
Generic subscriber registry for broadcasting to SSE clients.

Supports channel-based filtering — subscribers connect with optional
channel filters, and broadcasts reach only matching subscribers.

Two identity slots enable both targeted disconnection and per-scope cap
enforcement:
- `scope` — a single capped identity (e.g., session hash). Subject to
  the per-scope cap and matched by `close_by_identity`. Use for the
  narrowest identity the subscriber belongs to.
- `groups` — any number of uncapped identities (e.g., account id).
  Matched by `close_by_identity` but not subject to any cap. Use for
  coarser scopes a stream should be reachable by.

The split keeps "tabs-per-session" cap semantics sane when a stream also
carries a broader identity for coarse close — the broader identity
doesn't cap across sessions.

declarations.name: create_deno_runtime
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a runtime backed by Deno APIs.

Returns an object satisfying all `*Deps` interfaces from `runtime/deps.ts`.
Pass to shared functions that accept `EnvDeps`, `FsReadDeps`, etc.
declarations.return_description: runtime implementation using Deno APIs

modules.module_comment:
Deno implementation of `RuntimeDeps`.

Provides `create_deno_runtime(args)` — a factory returning a `RuntimeDeps`
backed by Deno APIs. Only imported by Deno entry points (compiled binaries
for tx, zzz, etc.).

declarations.name: StatResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result of a stat operation.

declarations.name: CommandResult
declarations.doc_comment:
Result of executing a command.

`timed_out` is present only when `timeout_ms` was passed in `RunCommandOptions`
and the process was killed after exceeding the timeout. Callers that pass
`timeout_ms` should check this flag to distinguish timeout from exit-code failure.

declarations.name: RunCommandOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `run_command`.

properties.name: cwd
properties.doc_comment: Working directory for the child process.

properties.name: signal
properties.doc_comment: AbortSignal to terminate the child process.

properties.name: timeout_ms
properties.doc_comment: Kill the process and return `timed_out: true` after this many milliseconds.

declarations.name: EnvDeps
declarations.doc_comment: Environment variable access.

properties.name: env_get
properties.doc_comment: Get an environment variable value.

properties.name: env_set
properties.doc_comment: Set an environment variable.

declarations.name: ReadTextFromOffsetResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result of reading text from a byte offset.

properties.name: content
properties.doc_comment: Decoded text content read from the offset.

properties.name: bytes_read
properties.doc_comment: Number of bytes actually read.

properties.name: file_size
properties.doc_comment: Total file size at the time of the read (for truncation detection).

declarations.name: FsReadDeps
declarations.doc_comment: File system read operations.

properties.name: stat
properties.doc_comment: Get file/directory stats, or null if path doesn't exist.

properties.name: read_text_file
properties.doc_comment: Read a file as text. Throws if the file does not exist.

properties.name: read_file
properties.doc_comment: Read a file as bytes. Throws if the file does not exist.

properties.name: read_text_from_offset
properties.doc_comment:
Read text starting from a byte offset. Throws if the file does not exist.

Returns `content`, `bytes_read`, and `file_size` so callers can detect
truncation (when `file_size < offset`) and tail incrementally without
re-reading the whole file.

properties.name: readdir
properties.doc_comment: List directory entries (names, not full paths). Throws if the directory does not exist.

declarations.name: FsWriteDeps
declarations.doc_comment: File system write operations.

properties.name: mkdir
properties.doc_comment: Create a directory.

properties.name: write_text_file
properties.doc_comment: Write text to a file.

properties.name: write_file
properties.doc_comment: Write bytes to a file.

properties.name: rename
properties.doc_comment: Rename (move) a file.

declarations.name: FsRemoveDeps
declarations.doc_comment: File system remove operations.

properties.name: remove
properties.doc_comment: Remove a file or directory.

declarations.name: CommandDeps
declarations.doc_comment: Command execution.

properties.name: run_command
properties.type_signature:
(
		cmd: string,
		args: Array<string>,
		options?: RunCommandOptions,
	) => Promise<CommandResult>
properties.doc_comment:
Run a command and return the result. Never throws — failures surface as
`success: false`.

`options.cwd` sets the child's working directory. `options.signal` aborts
the child when the signal fires. `options.timeout_ms` kills the child
after the given duration and returns `timed_out: true` on the result.

declarations.name: FetchDeps
declarations.doc_comment: HTTP fetch capability.

properties.name: fetch
properties.doc_comment: Fetch a URL. Same signature as the global `fetch`.

declarations.name: LogDeps
declarations.doc_comment: Warning/diagnostic output.

properties.name: warn
properties.doc_comment: Log a warning message.

declarations.name: TerminalDeps
declarations.doc_comment: Terminal I/O operations.

properties.name: stdout_write
properties.doc_comment: Write bytes to stdout.

properties.name: stdin_read
properties.doc_comment: Read bytes from stdin, or null on EOF.

declarations.name: ProcessDeps
declarations.doc_comment: Process lifecycle.

properties.name: exit
properties.doc_comment: Exit the process with a code.

declarations.name: RuntimeDeps
declarations.doc_comment:
Full runtime capabilities returned by `create_deno_runtime` or `create_node_runtime`.

Extends all `*Deps` interfaces with additional app-level capabilities.
Functions should accept narrow `*Deps` interfaces, not this full type —
this type is for the wiring layer that creates and passes the runtime.

properties.name: env_all
properties.doc_comment: Get all environment variables.

properties.name: args
properties.doc_comment: CLI arguments passed to the program.

properties.name: cwd
properties.doc_comment: Get current working directory.

properties.name: run_command_inherit
properties.doc_comment: Run a command with inherited stdout/stderr (output goes directly to terminal).

modules.module_comment:
Shared dependency interfaces for runtime operations.

Small composable interfaces that functions accept for only the capabilities
they need. Both Deno and Node implementations satisfy all these interfaces
via `RuntimeDeps`.

declarations.name: write_file_atomic
declarations.doc_comment:
Write a file atomically via temp file + rename.

Writes to `<path>.tmp` then renames over `path` so readers either see the
old contents or the full new contents — never a partial write.

throws.description: if `write_text_file` or `rename` rejects (permissions, disk full, cross-device rename, etc.)

declarations.type_signature: (deps: Pick<FsWriteDeps, "write_text_file" | "rename">, path: string, content: string): Promise<void>

modules.module_comment: File system utilities.

declarations.name: MockRuntime
declarations.doc_comment: Mock `RuntimeDeps` with observable state for assertions.

properties.name: mock_env
properties.doc_comment: Mock environment variables.

properties.name: mock_fs

properties.name: mock_fs_bytes

properties.name: mock_dirs

properties.name: exit_calls

properties.name: command_calls
properties.type_signature: Array<{cmd: string; args: Array<string>; options?: RunCommandOptions}>
