properties.name: command_inherit_calls
properties.doc_comment: Commands executed with inherit.

properties.name: stdout_writes
properties.doc_comment: Stdout writes recorded.

properties.name: mock_command_results
properties.doc_comment: Mock command results (cmd -> result).

properties.name: stdin_buffer
properties.doc_comment: Stdin buffer for input simulation.

properties.name: fetch_calls
properties.type_signature: Array<{input: string | URL | Request; init?: RequestInit}>
properties.doc_comment: Fetch calls recorded.

properties.name: mock_fetch_responses
properties.doc_comment: Mock fetch responses (URL substring -> Response).

declarations.name: create_mock_runtime
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a mock `RuntimeDeps` for testing.

The mock `exit` records the code on `exit_calls` and throws `MockExitError`
(so the never-returning contract holds in tests). `fetch` throws `TypeError`
when no `mock_fetch_responses` pattern matches the request URL.
declarations.return_description: `MockRuntime` with controllable state

declarations.name: reset_mock_runtime
declarations.doc_comment: Reset a mock runtime to initial state.

declarations.name: set_mock_stdin
declarations.doc_comment: Set stdin buffer for simulating user input.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: string to provide as stdin input

declarations.name: MockExitError
declarations.doc_comment:
Error thrown when mock `runtime.exit()` is called.

Tests can catch this to verify exit behavior.

modules.module_comment:
Mock `RuntimeDeps` for testing.

Provides a fully controllable runtime implementation for unit tests.
Consumer projects can extend `MockRuntime` with project-specific helpers
(e.g. `setup_mock_zap_config`) that stay local.

declarations.name: create_node_runtime
declarations.doc_comment: Create a `RuntimeDeps` backed by Node.js APIs.
declarations.return_description: `RuntimeDeps` implementation using Node.js runtime

modules.module_comment:
Node.js implementation of `RuntimeDeps`.

Provides the same interface as `runtime/deno.ts` but backed by Node.js APIs.
Used for running servers in Node.js and for tests (vitest runs in Node).

declarations.name: SchemaFieldMeta
declarations.doc_comment: Zod `.meta()` shape for fuz_app schema metadata conventions.

properties.name: sensitivity
properties.doc_comment: Sensitivity level for masking/redaction. `'secret'` masks the value.

modules.module_comment:
Shared Zod `.meta()` conventions for fuz_app schemas.

Cross-cutting metadata shape used by env schemas, auth input schemas,
surface generation, and test helpers.

declarations.name: Sensitivity
declarations.doc_comment:
Sensitivity level for a schema field.

- `'secret'` — value is masked in logs and UI (e.g. passwords, API keys, signing keys)

modules.module_comment:
Sensitivity levels for schema metadata.

Used by env schemas and auth input schemas to control masking/redaction
in startup logs, surface explorers, and test output.

declarations.name: AppBackend
declarations.doc_comment:
Result of `create_app_backend()` — database metadata + deps bundle.

This is the initialized backend, not the HTTP server.
Pass it to `create_app_server()` to assemble the Hono app.

properties.name: migration_results
properties.doc_comment: Migration results from `create_app_backend` — auth migrations plus any consumer namespaces passed via `migration_namespaces`.

properties.name: close
properties.doc_comment: Close the database connection. Bound to the actual driver.

declarations.name: AuditFactory
declarations.doc_comment:
Callback that builds the bound `AuditEmitter` after the backend's pool
`Db` and `Logger` exist. Required on `CreateAppBackendOptions` so the
consumer owns subscriber-chain composition and `AuditLogConfig`
selection without the factory holding a default.

The factory is invoked exactly once during `create_app_backend`, after
`create_db` resolves and migrations run. The emitter it returns lands
on `AppDeps.audit` and is captured by every query/handler that reaches
`deps.audit.emit(...)`.

The canonical body is a one-liner over `create_audit_emitter`:

```ts
audit_factory: ({db, log}) => create_audit_emitter({
  db,
  log,
  on_audit_event,
  audit_log_config,
})
```

Returning an emitter built against a different `db` than the one passed
in would route audit writes to a different pool than handlers query —
the callback shape exists specifically to make that mistake structurally
impossible.

declarations.name: default_audit_factory
declarations.doc_comment:
Trivial `AuditFactory` for consumers that don't compose `on_audit_event`
or `audit_log_config`. Equivalent to
`({db, log}) => create_audit_emitter({db, log})` — exported so the
default case stays a single-symbol reference rather than five tokens
of boilerplate at every consumer.

Use the inline form when you need to thread `on_audit_event` /
`audit_log_config` / `emit_decorator`; the factory composes those
three fields itself so there's nothing this constant can pass through.

declarations.name: CreateAppBackendOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Input for `create_app_backend()`.

`keyring` is passed pre-validated — callers handle their own error reporting
(e.g., tx uses `runtime.exit(1)` on invalid keys).

properties.name: stat
properties.doc_comment: Get file/directory stats, or null if path doesn't exist.

properties.name: read_text_file
properties.doc_comment: Read a file as text.

properties.name: delete_file
properties.doc_comment: Delete a file.

properties.name: database_url
properties.doc_comment: Database connection URL (`postgres://`, `file://`, or `memory://`).

properties.name: keyring
properties.doc_comment: Validated cookie signing keyring.

properties.name: password
properties.doc_comment: Password hashing implementation. Use `argon2_password_deps` in production.

properties.name: log
properties.doc_comment: Structured logger instance. Omit for default (`new Logger('server')`).

properties.name: audit_factory
properties.doc_comment:
Build the bound `AuditEmitter` once the backend's pool `Db` + `Logger`
exist. Required — the factory owns subscriber-chain composition and
`AuditLogConfig` selection without `create_app_backend` holding a
default. Typical body:

```ts
audit_factory: ({db, log}) => create_audit_emitter({
  db,
  log,
  on_audit_event,
  audit_log_config,
})
```

Additional listeners (factory-managed audit SSE, per-endpoint WS
auth guards) are appended at `create_app_server` time via
`audit.on_event_chain.push(...)`.

properties.name: migration_namespaces
properties.doc_comment:
Additional migration namespaces to run after the builtin auth namespace.
The shared `schema_version` table records one row per applied migration
(`namespace`, `name`, `sequence`); order is append-only so forward-only
guarantees hold per-namespace.

Names in `reserved_migration_namespaces` (currently `['fuz_auth']`) are
rejected at startup. Omit for no extra namespaces. This is the only
place to splice consumer migrations — DB init belongs to the backend
lifecycle, not server assembly.

declarations.name: create_app_backend
declarations.doc_comment:
Initialize the backend: database + auth migrations + deps.

Calls `create_db` → `run_migrations` (auth namespace, then any
`migration_namespaces` from options in order) → `audit_factory({db, log})`
and bundles the result with the provided keyring and password deps.

throws.description: if `migration_namespaces` contains a namespace in `reserved_migration_namespaces`

declarations.return_description: app backend with deps, database metadata, and combined migration results

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: keyring, password deps, `audit_factory`, optional database URL, and optional `migration_namespaces`

modules.module_comment:
App backend types and factory — database initialization + auth migrations + deps.

Provides `AppBackend`, `CreateAppBackendOptions`, and `create_app_backend()`.

**Vocabulary**:
- `AppDeps` — stateless capabilities: injectable, swappable per environment
- `*Options` — static values set at startup, per-factory configuration
- Runtime state — mutable values (e.g., `bootstrap_status`) — NOT in deps or options

declarations.name: EffectErrorContext
declarations.doc_comment: Context passed to `on_effect_error` when a pending effect rejects.

properties.name: method
properties.doc_comment: HTTP method of the request that spawned the effect.

properties.name: path
properties.doc_comment: URL path of the request that spawned the effect.

declarations.name: BootstrapServerOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Bootstrap configuration for `AppServerOptions.bootstrap`.

Discriminated union over three deployment intents. Distinct from
`BootstrapRouteOptions` in `auth/bootstrap_routes.ts` — that one is
per-factory runtime state (mutable `bootstrap_status` ref, rate
limiter); this one is the consumer-facing server option that
`create_app_server` reads at startup to decide whether to mount the
routes and where.

Three modes:
- `disabled` — no route mounted, nothing in `/api/surface`.
  Equivalent to omitting `bootstrap` entirely; the explicit mode is
  for documentation and reviewable intent at the wiring layer.
- `surface_only` — route present, permanent 403 via
  `check_bootstrap_status`. For tests asserting on the
  disabled-but-present wire shape.
- `live` — route mounted, real token verification. Success path
  reachable. `token_path` is required (non-nullable).

properties.name: route_prefix
properties.doc_comment: Route prefix for surface generation. Default `'/api/account'`.

properties.name: route_prefix
properties.doc_comment: Route prefix for bootstrap routes. Default `'/api/account'`.

properties.name: on_bootstrap
properties.doc_comment:
Called after successful bootstrap (account + session created).
Use for app-specific post-bootstrap work like generating API tokens.

declarations.name: AppServerOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Configuration for `create_app_server()`.

Requires a pre-initialized `AppBackend` from `create_app_backend()`.
Two explicit steps: init backend then assemble server.

properties.name: backend
properties.doc_comment: Pre-initialized backend from `create_app_backend()`.

properties.name: session_options
properties.doc_comment: Session options for cookie-based auth.

properties.name: allowed_origins
properties.doc_comment: Parsed allowed origin patterns.

properties.name: proxy
properties.doc_comment: Trusted proxy options.

properties.name: ip_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment:
Shared IP rate limiter for login, bootstrap, and bearer auth.
Omit or `undefined` to use a default limiter (5 attempts per 15 minutes).
Pass `null` to explicitly disable rate limiting.
Also available on `AppServerContext` for route factory callbacks.

properties.name: login_account_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment:
Per-account rate limiter for login attempts.
Omit or `undefined` to use a default limiter (10 attempts per 30 minutes).
Pass `null` to explicitly disable rate limiting.
Also available on `AppServerContext` for route factory callbacks.

properties.name: signup_account_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment:
Per-account rate limiter for signup attempts, keyed by submitted username.
Omit or `undefined` to use a default limiter (10 attempts per 30 minutes).
Pass `null` to explicitly disable rate limiting.
Also available on `AppServerContext` for route factory callbacks.

properties.name: bearer_ip_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment:
Rate limiter for bearer token auth attempts (per-IP).
Omit or `undefined` to use a default limiter (5 attempts per 15 minutes).
Pass `null` to explicitly disable rate limiting.

properties.name: action_ip_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment:
Per-IP rate limiter for the action dispatchers (HTTP RPC + WebSocket).
Consulted for actions whose spec declares `rate_limit: 'ip'` or `'both'`.
Same limiter applies across transports — one budget per action.
Omit or `undefined` to use a default limiter (600 attempts per
15 minutes — permissive). Pass `null` to explicitly disable.
Also available on `AppServerContext` for consumers wiring
`register_action_ws`.

properties.name: action_account_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment:
Per-actor rate limiter for the action dispatchers (HTTP RPC + WebSocket).
Consulted for actions whose spec declares `rate_limit: 'account'` or
`'both'`. Keyed on `request_context.actor.id` (post-auth).
Omit or `undefined` to use a default limiter (1200 attempts per
15 minutes — permissive). Pass `null` to explicitly disable.
Also available on `AppServerContext` for consumers wiring
`register_action_ws`.

properties.name: max_body_size
properties.doc_comment:
Maximum allowed request body size in bytes.
Omit or `undefined` to use the default (1 MiB).
Pass `null` to explicitly disable body size limiting.

properties.name: daemon_token_state
properties.doc_comment: Daemon token state for keeper auth. Omit to disable.

properties.name: bootstrap
properties.doc_comment: Bootstrap options. Omit to skip bootstrap status check and routes.

properties.name: surface_route
properties.doc_comment:
Set to `false` to disable the auto-created surface route (`GET /api/surface`).
Default: auto-created (authenticated).

properties.name: create_route_specs
properties.doc_comment:
Build route specs from the initialized backend.
Called after all middleware is ready.

properties.name: transform_middleware
properties.doc_comment: Optional: transform middleware specs before applying.

properties.name: audit_log_sse
properties.doc_comment:
Enable factory-managed audit log SSE.

When truthy, creates an `AuditLogSse` instance internally, appends the SSE
listener to `backend.deps.audit.on_event_chain` (composing with the
consumer's `on_audit_event` callback rather than rebuilding `AppDeps`), and
auto-includes `audit_log_event_specs` in the surface. The result is exposed
on `AppServerContext` (for route factories) and `AppServer` (for the caller),
always typed as `AuditLogSse | null` — when this option is set, the field
is non-null. Use `require_audit_sse(ctx)` to assert the invariant in
route factories that depend on it.

Pass `true` for defaults (admin role), or `{role: 'custom'}` for a custom role.
Omit to wire audit SSE manually.

properties.name: event_specs
properties.doc_comment: SSE event specs for surface generation. Defaults to `[]` (no SSE events).

properties.name: rpc_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
RPC endpoint specs — single source of truth for both surface generation
*and* live dispatch. Each entry is mounted via `create_rpc_endpoint`
against the assembled Hono app, so consumers no longer call
`create_rpc_endpoint` themselves inside `create_route_specs`.

Accepts either an array (evaluated eagerly) or a factory
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => Array<RpcEndpointSpec>` (evaluated after the
server context is assembled). Use the factory form when action lists
depend on `ctx.deps` / `ctx.app_settings` — e.g.
`create_standard_rpc_actions(ctx.deps, {app_settings: ctx.app_settings})`.

properties.name: upgradeWebSocket
properties.doc_comment:
Hono adapter's `upgradeWebSocket` helper. Required whenever
`ws_endpoints` resolves to a non-empty array — `create_app_server`
throws at assembly otherwise. Omit (along with `ws_endpoints`)
when the consumer doesn't mount any WS endpoints. The same
adapter helper services every `WsEndpointSpec` mounted from
`ws_endpoints` — one adapter per app.

For Node, `import {upgradeWebSocket} from '@hono/node-ws'`. For
Deno, `import {upgradeWebSocket} from 'hono/deno'`. Test harnesses
use `create_stub_upgrade` from `$lib/testing/ws_round_trip.ts`.

properties.name: ws_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
WebSocket endpoint specs — single source of truth for both surface
generation *and* live dispatch. Each entry is auto-mounted via
`register_ws_endpoint` against the assembled Hono app, so
consumers no longer call `register_ws_endpoint` themselves.

Accepts either an array (evaluated eagerly) or a factory
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => ReadonlyArray<WsEndpointSpec>`
(evaluated after the server context is assembled). Use the factory
form when action lists depend on `ctx.deps` /
`ctx.action_*_rate_limiter` — e.g. when spreading
`create_standard_rpc_actions(ctx.deps, ...)` over WS.

When non-empty, `upgradeWebSocket` must be supplied (throws
otherwise). A factory returning `[]` does NOT trip the check —
feature-flag gated WS surfaces stay safe.

Duplicate `path` values across two `WsEndpointSpec`s throw at
mount time (Hono would silently shadow them otherwise).

Each spec's `auth_guard?` defaults to `true` — the factory
composes `create_ws_auth_guard` + `create_ws_logout_closer`
against the mounted transport and appends them to
`deps.audit.on_event_chain`. Wiring is deduped by transport
**reference identity** so two specs sharing one
`BackendWebsocketTransport` instance get a single pair of
listeners; wrapped / proxied transports dedupe as separate
entries (set `auth_guard: false` on duplicates and compose
against the underlying transport once).

properties.name: env_schema
properties.doc_comment:
Env schema for surface generation. Defaults to `BaseServerEnv` —
pass an extended schema (typically `BaseServerEnv.extend({...})`)
when the consumer adds app-specific env vars.

properties.name: post_route_middleware
properties.doc_comment: Middleware applied after routes, before static serving. Included in surface.

properties.name: static_serving
properties.doc_comment: Static file serving. Omit if not serving static files.

properties.name: await_pending_effects
properties.doc_comment:
Await all pending fire-and-forget effects before returning the response.
Use in tests so audit log assertions don't need polling.
Default `false` (production: true fire-and-forget).

properties.name: on_effect_error
properties.doc_comment:
Called when a pending effect rejects.
Use for monitoring, metrics, or alerting in production.
Only called when `await_pending_effects` is `false` (production mode).

properties.name: env_values
properties.doc_comment: Env values for startup summary logging.

declarations.name: AppServerContext
declarations.doc_comment: Context passed to `create_route_specs`.

properties.name: ip_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment: Shared IP rate limiter (from options). `null` when not configured.

properties.name: login_account_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment: Per-account login rate limiter (from options). `null` when not configured.

properties.name: signup_account_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment: Per-account signup rate limiter (from options). `null` when not configured.

properties.name: action_ip_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment: Per-IP action-dispatcher rate limiter — shared across HTTP RPC + WS. `null` when not configured.

properties.name: action_account_rate_limiter
properties.doc_comment: Per-actor action-dispatcher rate limiter — shared across HTTP RPC + WS. `null` when not configured.

properties.name: app_settings
properties.doc_comment: Global app settings (mutable ref — mutated by settings admin route).

properties.name: audit_sse
properties.doc_comment:
Factory-managed audit log SSE. Non-null when the `audit_log_sse`
option was passed to `create_app_server`, `null` when omitted.
Use `require_audit_sse(ctx)` to assert the invariant.

declarations.name: AppServer
declarations.doc_comment: Result of `create_app_server()`.

properties.name: surface_spec
properties.doc_comment: Surface spec — serializable surface + raw specs that produced it.

properties.name: app_settings
properties.doc_comment: Global app settings (mutable ref — mutated by settings admin route).

properties.name: migration_results
properties.doc_comment: Migration results from `create_app_backend` (auth + any `migration_namespaces` passed there).

properties.name: audit_sse
properties.doc_comment:
Factory-managed audit log SSE. Non-null when the `audit_log_sse`
option was passed to `create_app_server`, `null` when omitted.
Use `require_audit_sse(server)` to assert the invariant.

properties.name: ws_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
Path-keyed map of mounted WS endpoints. Each value is the
`BackendWebsocketTransport` `create_app_server` registered
connections against — supplied via `WsEndpointSpec.transport` or
auto-created when omitted. Retain for broadcast / fan-out:

```ts
app_server.ws_endpoints['/api/ws'].send_to_account(account_id, msg);
```

Empty when no `ws_endpoints` were mounted.

properties.name: close
properties.doc_comment: Close the database connection. Propagated from `AppBackend`.

declarations.name: require_audit_sse
declarations.doc_comment:
Assert that `audit_sse` was wired by `create_app_server` and return it
as a non-null `AuditLogSse`. Throws a labelled error when the
`audit_log_sse` option was not passed to `create_app_server`.

Use in route factories that depend on factory-managed audit SSE:

```ts
create_route_specs: (ctx) => create_audit_log_route_specs({
  stream: require_audit_sse(ctx),
}),
```

Preferred over `ctx.audit_sse!` — `!` lies to the type system and
produces a downstream cannot-read-property crash if a consumer wires
the route without enabling the option.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_MAX_BODY_SIZE
declarations.doc_comment: Default maximum request body size: 1 MiB.

declarations.name: create_app_server
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a fully assembled Hono app with auth, middleware, and routes.

Handles the assembly lifecycle: proxy middleware → auth middleware →
bootstrap status → route specs → surface generation → Hono app assembly →
static serving. Database migrations belong to the backend lifecycle —
pass `migration_namespaces` to `create_app_backend`.

When `audit_log_sse` is set, the SSE registry's listener is appended to
`backend.deps.audit.on_event_chain` — no shallow-copy of `AppDeps`. The
`audit_sse` field on the returned `AppServer` (and the
`AppServerContext` passed to `create_route_specs`) is non-null in that
case; consumers can call `require_audit_sse(ctx)` / `require_audit_sse(server)`
to assert the invariant.
declarations.return_description: assembled Hono app, backend, surface build, and bootstrap status

modules.module_comment:
Server assembly factory.

Consumers provide a pre-initialized `AppBackend` and options (session,
origins, routes); `create_app_server()` handles middleware, bootstrap
status, surface generation, and Hono app assembly.

declarations.name: BaseServerEnv
declarations.doc_comment:
Base Zod schema for server environment variables.

Provides the common fields used by fuz apps:
server config, database, auth, security, public URLs, and SMTP.

Apps can use directly or extend with app-specific fields via `.extend()`.
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ NODE_ENV: ZodEnum<{ development: "development"; production: "production"; }>; PORT: ZodDefault<ZodCoercedNumber<unknown>>; HOST: ZodDefault<ZodString>; ... 9 more ...; SMTP_PASSWORD: ZodOptional<...>; }, $strict>

declarations.name: ServerEnvOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Validated server env config — the artifacts `create_app_server()` needs.

declarations.name: ServerEnvOptionsError
declarations.doc_comment: Error from `validate_server_env` — keyring or origin validation failed.

declarations.name: validate_server_env
declarations.doc_comment:
Validate a loaded `BaseServerEnv` and produce the artifacts needed for server init.

Handles keyring validation, origin parsing, and bootstrap token path extraction.
Returns a Result so callers handle errors their own way (exit, logging, etc).
declarations.type_signature: (env: { NODE_ENV: "development" | "production"; PORT: number; HOST: string; DATABASE_URL: string; SECRET_FUZ_COOKIE_KEYS: string; FUZ_ALLOWED_ORIGINS: string; PUBLIC_FUZ_API_URL: string; ... 5 more ...; SMTP_PASSWORD?: string | undefined; }): ServerEnvOptionsResult
declarations.return_description: `{ok: true, keyring, allowed_origins, bootstrap_token_path}` or `{ok: false, field, errors}`

parameters.name: env
parameters.type: { NODE_ENV: "development" | "production"; PORT: number; HOST: string; DATABASE_URL: string; SECRET_FUZ_COOKIE_KEYS: string; FUZ_ALLOWED_ORIGINS: string; PUBLIC_FUZ_API_URL: string; PUBLIC_FUZ_WEBSOCKET_URL?: string | undefined; ... 4 more ...; SMTP_PASSWORD?: string | undefined; }

modules.module_comment:
Base server environment schema and validation.

Provides `BaseServerEnv` — a shared Zod schema for common server env vars
that apps can use directly or extend with app-specific fields.

Generic env loading lives in `env/load.ts`.

declarations.name: log_startup_summary
declarations.doc_comment:
Log a startup summary from an `AppSurface`.

Logs route count, middleware count, env breakdown (when non-empty),
and event/channel counts (when non-empty). When `env_values` is provided,
non-secret values are logged and secrets are masked with `***`.
declarations.type_signature: (surface: AppSurface, log: Logger, env_values?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined): void

parameters.name: env_values
parameters.description: optional env values to log (secrets are masked)

modules.module_comment:
Composable startup summary helpers.

Logs a human-readable summary from an `AppSurface`.

declarations.name: ServeStaticOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `serve_static` factory functions (matches Hono's `serveStatic` signature).

declarations.name: ServeStaticFactory
declarations.doc_comment:
Factory function that creates a static file serving middleware.

Matches the signature of `serveStatic` from `hono/deno` and `@hono/node-server/serve-static`.

declarations.name: create_static_middleware
declarations.doc_comment:
Create static file serving middleware for SvelteKit static builds.

Returns an array of middleware handlers to register on `'/*'`.
declarations.type_signature: (serve_static: ServeStaticFactory, options?: { root?: string | undefined; spa_fallback?: string | undefined; is_spa_route?: ((path: string) => boolean) | undefined; } | undefined): MiddlewareHandler[]

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { root?: string | undefined; spa_fallback?: string | undefined; is_spa_route?: ((path: string) => boolean) | undefined; } | undefined
parameters.description: optional root directory and SPA fallback path

modules.module_comment:
Static file serving middleware for SvelteKit static builds.

Multi-step static serving:
- Step 1: Exact path match (handles /, assets, images)
- Step 2: `.html` fallback for clean URLs (`/about` → `/about.html`)
- Step 3 (optional): SPA fallback for client-side routes

declarations.name: NginxValidationResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result of validating an nginx config template string.

properties.name: ok
properties.doc_comment: True when no errors were detected. Warnings do not affect this flag.

properties.name: warnings
properties.doc_comment: Non-fatal issues — missing optional headers, weakened defaults, etc.

properties.name: errors
properties.doc_comment: Fatal issues — missing `/api` block, missing required security headers, etc.

declarations.name: validate_nginx_config
declarations.doc_comment:
Validate an nginx config template string for security properties.

Checks for required security headers, Authorization stripping in `/api`
blocks, and the nginx `add_header` inheritance gotcha. Designed for
fuz_app consumer deploy configs (zap.ts `NGINX_CONFIG` constants).

Limitations: string pattern matching, not a real nginx parser. Catches
common omissions in fuz_app deploy configs but won't catch all possible
misconfigurations.

modules.module_comment:
String-based nginx config validator for fuz_app deploy configs.

Checks consumer `NGINX_CONFIG` template strings for required security
properties. This is pattern matching on template strings, not a real
nginx parser — it catches common security omissions but won't catch
all possible misconfigurations.

declarations.name: StandardAdminIntegrationTestOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Configuration for `describe_standard_admin_integration_tests`.

properties.name: setup_test
properties.doc_comment:
Per-test fixture-producing function. The admin suite calls this in
every `test()` body — auth_integration_truncate_tables clears
`account`, so each test re-bootstraps the keeper.

properties.name: surface_source
properties.doc_comment:
App surface (with route specs + middleware specs) for route iteration
and error-coverage scoping. Constructed in TS by the consumer (same
shape for in-process and cross-process tests).

properties.name: capabilities
properties.doc_comment: Backend capability declarations.

properties.name: session_options
properties.doc_comment: Session config — needed for cookie_name + factory-form rpc_endpoints resolution.

properties.name: roles
properties.doc_comment: Role schema result from `create_role_schema()` — used to determine valid/invalid/web-grantable roles.

properties.name: rpc_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
RPC endpoint specs — the source `RpcAction` arrays. Required; role_grant
grant/revoke are RPC-only and the suite hard-fails without them.

properties.name: admin_prefix
properties.doc_comment:
Path prefix where admin routes are mounted (e.g., `'/api/admin'`).
Used by the 401/403 error-coverage probe to scope to fuz_app admin
routes only, avoiding app-specific admin-gated routes that may use
stub deps. Default `'/api/admin'`.

declarations.name: describe_standard_admin_integration_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Standard admin integration test suite for fuz_app admin routes.

Exercises account listing, role_grant grant/revoke (via RPC), session
management, token management, audit log reads, admin-to-admin
isolation, and 401/403 error-coverage on the admin REST surface.
Output-schema conformance is not in scope — see the module docstring
for the suites that cover it.

throws.description: at setup time when `options.rpc_endpoints` is empty — admin

modules.module_comment:
Standard admin integration test suite for fuz_app admin routes.

`describe_standard_admin_integration_tests` creates a composable test suite
that exercises admin account listing, role_grant grant/revoke (via the RPC
surface — see `role_grant_offer_create` / `role_grant_revoke`), session/token
management, and audit log routes against a real PGlite database.

Consumers call it with their route factory, session config, role schema,
and RPC endpoint specs — all admin route tests come for free.

Scope: admin *semantics* — cross-admin isolation, role_grant grant/revoke
flow, session/token revoke-all, audit writes. Output-schema conformance
for admin methods is **not** the concern of this suite; it lives in:

- `describe_rpc_round_trip_tests` — every RPC method (admin methods
  included) is hit with a spec-generated valid body and the 2xx result
  is validated against `spec.output`.
- `describe_round_trip_validation` — every REST route is hit and
  validated against its declared `output` / error schemas (SSE routes
  skipped via `Content-Type: text/event-stream`).
- `describe_sse_route_tests` — SSE frames validated against their
  declared `EventSpec`.

declarations.name: describe_adversarial_404
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate adversarial 404 response validation tests.

For each route with `params` + 404 in `error_schemas`:
1. Creates a stub handler returning 404 with the declared error code
2. Fires a request with valid-format params (nil UUIDs for UUID params)
3. Validates response status is 404
4. Validates response body matches the declared 404 Zod schema

modules.module_comment:
Adversarial 404 testing for routes with params and declared 404 error schemas.

Creates stub handlers that return 404 with the declared error code,
fires requests with valid-format-but-nonexistent params (nil UUIDs),
and validates response bodies against the declared 404 Zod schemas.

No DB needed — tests schema conformance of 404 responses, not real handlers.

declarations.name: AdversarialHeaderCase
declarations.doc_comment: A header-level attack case for middleware stack testing.

properties.name: expected_error_schema
properties.doc_comment: Zod schema to validate error response body against. Defaults to `ApiError` when `expected_error` is set.

properties.name: validate_expectation
properties.doc_comment: Whether the request should reach token validation or be short-circuited by earlier middleware.

declarations.name: create_standard_adversarial_cases
declarations.doc_comment:
7 standard adversarial header cases applicable to any middleware stack.

Origin verification is Origin-only — fuz_app's `verify_request_source`
no longer falls back to `Referer` (matches `zzz_server::auth::is_request_origin_allowed`).
Bearer auth still treats a `Referer` header as a browser-context
indicator and silently discards the bearer token — so Referer-bearing
requests reach the route as unauthenticated rather than 403.

parameters.name: allowed_origin
parameters.description: an origin that passes the origin check

declarations.name: describe_standard_adversarial_headers
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a middleware stack app with standard adversarial header tests.

Convenience wrapper combining `create_test_middleware_stack_app`
and `create_standard_adversarial_cases`. Generates one `test()` per case
inside a `describe()` block — asserts body content for both error and
success cases, and verifies that `mock_validate` was (or was not) reached
per the case's `validate_expectation` flag, ensuring earlier middleware
actually short-circuits before token validation in the rejection cases.
declarations.type_signature: (suite_name: string, options: TestMiddlewareStackOptions, allowed_origin: string, extra_cases?: AdversarialHeaderCase[] | undefined): void

parameters.name: allowed_origin
parameters.description: an origin that passes the origin check (used for standard cases)

parameters.name: extra_cases
parameters.description: additional cases appended after the 7 standard ones

modules.module_comment:
Adversarial header attack test suite.

Provides standard header injection test cases and a convenience wrapper
for exercising middleware stacks with adversarial headers.

declarations.name: generate_input_test_cases
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate adversarial test cases for a route's input schema.

Produces focused, non-redundant cases:
- Whole-body: send array instead of object, extra unknown key
- Missing required fields (without defaults)
- One wrong-type value per field
- Null for required non-nullable fields
- One format violation per constrained field

throws.description: if the seed body built by `generate_valid_value` fails

declarations.name: generate_params_test_cases
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate adversarial test cases for a route's params schema.

Params are always strings from URL segments. Only generates cases for
format-constrained fields (uuid, pattern) since unconstrained string
params accept any string value.

declarations.name: generate_query_test_cases
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate adversarial test cases for a route's query schema.

Query params are always strings from the URL. Generates cases for:
- Missing required fields
- Format violations on constrained fields (uuid, pattern)

declarations.name: describe_adversarial_input
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate adversarial input validation test suites.

Tests input body validation and params validation for all routes.
Uses correct auth credentials so auth guards pass and validation
middleware is actually exercised.

modules.module_comment:
Adversarial input validation testing for route specs.

Walks Zod schemas directly to generate payloads that must fail validation.
Fires requests against a test app and asserts 400 responses before handlers
are reached.

Tests are focused: one representative wrong-type value per field, one format
violation per constrained field, one null and one missing test per required
field, plus whole-body structural attacks (non-object body, extra unknown keys).

declarations.name: stub_password_deps
declarations.doc_comment:
Fast password stub for tests that don't exercise login/password flows.

Hashes are deterministic (`stub_hash_<password>`) and verify correctly,
so auth bootstrap and session creation work without Argon2 overhead.

declarations.name: TEST_COOKIE_SECRET
declarations.doc_comment: 64-hex-char test cookie secret — deterministic, never used in production.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_TEST_PASSWORD
declarations.doc_comment:
Default password for bootstrapped test accounts. Shared between the
in-process keeper bootstrap (`bootstrap_test_keeper`,
`create_test_account_with_credentials`, `create_test_app_server`,
`TestApp.create_account`) and the cross-process bootstrap
(`cross_backend/setup.ts`). The two paths MUST agree — when they
diverged during the 3d cross-process lift, ~20 login tests 401'd
silently against the cross-process backend because the per-test
fixture minted accounts under a different default than the
integration suite's hardcoded login bodies expected. Consumers
hardcoding the literal string in test bodies should import this
constant instead so a future divergence becomes a typecheck miss
rather than a runtime password mismatch.

declarations.name: CreateTestAccountWithCredentialsOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Options for `bootstrap_test_keeper` and `create_test_account_with_credentials`.

Same shape for both — the data inserted is identical; the only behavioral
difference is the lock flip on the keeper path.

declarations.name: BootstrapTestKeeperOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Alias for the keeper-flavored call site. Same shape.

declarations.name: create_test_account_with_credentials
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a test account with credentials. Use for additional accounts
minted alongside the keeper (e.g. `TestApp.create_account` for
cross-account / multi-user tests). Does NOT flip `bootstrap_lock` —
non-keeper accounts should not appear to the system as bootstrap
having happened.

Creates an account with actor, grants roles, creates an API token,
creates a session, and signs a session cookie.
declarations.type_signature: (options: CreateTestAccountWithCredentialsOptions): Promise<{ account: { id: string & $brand<"Uuid">; username: string; }; actor: { ...; }; api_token: string; session_cookie: string; }>
declarations.return_type: Promise<{ account: { id: string & $brand<"Uuid">; username: string; }; actor: { id: string & $brand<"Uuid">; }; api_token: string; session_cookie: string; }>

declarations.name: bootstrap_test_keeper
declarations.doc_comment:
Bootstrap the test-DB keeper. Direct-query shortcut for the default
`create_test_app` path — bootstrap is not what most tests exercise, so
we skip the real `bootstrap_account` flow (no audit row, no
`on_bootstrap` callback). Tests that need the full success-path flow
use `create_test_app_for_bootstrap` instead.

Flips `bootstrap_lock.bootstrapped = true` so the post-insert DB state
matches a real bootstrap completion — production code can trust the
lock as the single signal without a belt-and-suspenders
`query_account_has_any` defense.
declarations.type_signature: (options: CreateTestAccountWithCredentialsOptions): Promise<{ account: { id: string & $brand<"Uuid">; username: string; }; actor: { ...; }; api_token: string; session_cookie: string; }>
declarations.return_type: Promise<{ account: { id: string & $brand<"Uuid">; username: string; }; actor: { id: string & $brand<"Uuid">; }; api_token: string; session_cookie: string; }>

declarations.name: TestAppServer
declarations.doc_comment: An `AppBackend` with a bootstrapped account, API token, and session cookie.

properties.name: account
properties.doc_comment: The bootstrapped account.

properties.name: actor
properties.doc_comment: The actor linked to the account.

properties.name: api_token
properties.doc_comment: Raw API token for Bearer auth.

properties.name: session_cookie
properties.doc_comment: Signed session cookie value for cookie auth.

properties.name: keyring
properties.doc_comment: Keyring used for cookie signing — exposed for forging expired/tampered cookies in tests.

properties.name: cleanup
properties.doc_comment: Release test resources (no-op when DB is injected or factory-cached).

declarations.name: TestAppServerOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Configuration for `create_test_app_server`.

properties.name: session_options
properties.doc_comment: Session options — needed for cookie signing.

properties.name: db
properties.doc_comment: Existing database — skips internal DB creation when provided. Caller owns the DB lifecycle.

properties.name: db_type
properties.doc_comment: Database driver type — only used when `db` is provided. Default: `'pglite-memory'`.

properties.name: password
properties.doc_comment: Password implementation. Default: `stub_password_deps`. Pass `argon2_password_deps` for tests that exercise login.

properties.name: username
properties.doc_comment: Username for the bootstrapped account. Default: `'keeper'`.

properties.name: password_value
properties.doc_comment: Password for the bootstrapped account. Default: `DEFAULT_TEST_PASSWORD`.

properties.name: roles
properties.doc_comment: Roles to grant. Default: `[ROLE_KEEPER]`.

properties.name: audit_factory
properties.doc_comment:
Build the bound `AuditEmitter` used by the test backend. Defaults to
`default_audit_factory` (a no-listener `create_audit_emitter` over
the test backend's `{db, log}`). Pass a custom factory when a test
needs:
- to capture audit events (compose `on_audit_event` inside the body)
- to register consumer event-type schemas (pass `audit_log_config`)
- to instrument `emit` ordering (`create_emit_ordering_audit_factory`)
- to wrap or replace the emitter for some other reason

Matches the production shape — `create_app_backend` requires an
`audit_factory` and `create_test_app_server` mirrors that contract
end-to-end. The earlier `on_audit_event` / `audit_log_config` sugar
fields were removed alongside the `CreateAppBackendOptions` rename.

declarations.name: CreateTestAppOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Configuration for `create_test_app`.

properties.name: create_route_specs
properties.doc_comment: Route spec factory — called with the assembled `AppServerContext`.

properties.name: rpc_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
RPC endpoints mounted by `create_app_server` — eager array or
`(ctx: AppServerContext) => Array<RpcEndpointSpec>` factory. Single
source of truth; the equivalent slot under `app_options` is `Omit`'d
so setup-time path lookup and runtime dispatch read from one place.
Symmetric with the suite-level `rpc_endpoints` option on
`describe_standard_admin_integration_tests` etc.

properties.name: bootstrap
properties.doc_comment:
Bootstrap config — symmetric with `AppServerOptions.bootstrap`. Same
single-source-of-truth precedent as `rpc_endpoints`: setup-time surface
generation and runtime dispatch both read this slot, so the equivalent
field under `app_options` is `Omit`'d. Discriminated union over
`{mode: 'disabled' | 'surface_only' | 'live'}`. Omit (or pass
`{mode: 'disabled'}`) for the default — no bootstrap route mounted.

For tests that exercise the bootstrap success path against a real
token + empty DB, use `create_test_app_for_bootstrap` instead — it
skips the keeper pre-creation that blocks the success branch.

properties.name: app_options
properties.doc_comment:
Optional overrides for `AppServerOptions`. Excludes fields
`create_test_app` manages directly: `backend`, `session_options`,
`create_route_specs`, `rpc_endpoints`, `bootstrap` (top-level slots
above).

declarations.name: SuiteAppOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
`app_options` shape accepted by `create_test_app` and the DB-backed suite
helpers. Excludes fields the helpers manage directly — `backend` /
`session_options` / `create_route_specs` are constructed by the helper
itself; `rpc_endpoints` and `bootstrap` live on top-level options so
setup-time surface lookup and runtime dispatch read from one source of
truth.

declarations.name: TestAccount
declarations.doc_comment: A bootstrapped test account with credentials.

properties.name: session_cookie
properties.doc_comment: Signed session cookie value.

properties.name: api_token
properties.doc_comment: Raw API token for Bearer auth.

properties.name: create_session_headers
properties.doc_comment: Build request headers with this account's session cookie.

properties.name: create_bearer_headers
properties.doc_comment: Build request headers with this account's Bearer token.

declarations.name: TestApp
declarations.doc_comment: A fully assembled test app — Hono app + backend + helpers.

properties.name: create_session_headers
properties.doc_comment: Build request headers with the bootstrapped session cookie.

properties.name: create_bearer_headers
properties.doc_comment: Build request headers with the bootstrapped Bearer token.

properties.name: create_daemon_token_headers
properties.doc_comment: Build request headers with the daemon token (keeper auth).

properties.name: create_account
properties.doc_comment: Create an additional account with credentials.

properties.name: cleanup
properties.doc_comment: Cleanup resources (delegates to TestAppServer.cleanup).

declarations.name: create_test_app
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a fully assembled test app with a Hono server, middleware, and routes.

Combines `create_test_app_server` + `create_app_server` into a single call.
Disables rate limiters and logging by default (test-friendly).

A fresh Hono app is created each call — middleware closures bind to the
server's deps (db, keyring), so reuse across servers is unsafe.
The expensive resource (PGlite WASM) is cached separately in `test_db.ts`.
declarations.return_description: a `TestApp` ready for HTTP testing

declarations.name: CreateTestAppForBootstrapOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Configuration for `create_test_app_for_bootstrap`. Like
`CreateTestAppOptions` but the keeper-related fields drop (no
pre-bootstrap keeper) and `bootstrap` is required + narrowed to
`live` mode (the helper exists specifically to drive the success
path).

properties.name: bootstrap
properties.doc_comment: Live bootstrap config — the test drives `POST /bootstrap` against this.

properties.name: bootstrap_token
properties.doc_comment:
Token contents the stub fs returns when reading `bootstrap.token_path`.
The test posts a body containing this same value as `token` to satisfy
the timing-safe equality check inside `bootstrap_account`.

declarations.name: TestAppForBootstrap
declarations.doc_comment:
A fully assembled test app in the pre-bootstrap state — empty DB,
`bootstrap_lock.bootstrapped = false`, no keeper account. Test drives
`POST /bootstrap` itself.

properties.name: create_request_headers
properties.doc_comment: Build host/origin request headers for the anonymous bootstrap POST.

properties.name: cleanup
properties.doc_comment: Release test resources (no-op when DB is injected or factory-cached).

declarations.name: create_test_app_for_bootstrap
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a test app in the pre-bootstrap state for exercising the
bootstrap success path end-to-end.

Skips the keeper pre-creation `create_test_app` does by default —
`bootstrap_lock.bootstrapped` stays at `false` and the DB has no
accounts. The fs stubs return `options.bootstrap_token` when the
bootstrap handler reads `bootstrap.token_path`, so a `POST /bootstrap`
with `{token: bootstrap_token, username, password}` reaches the
success branch.

Pair with `describe_bootstrap_success_tests` for the consumer-runnable
suite that drives the full happy path + adjacent assertions on
observable state (account exists, audit row emitted, on_bootstrap
callback fired).
declarations.return_description: a `TestAppForBootstrap` ready for the test to drive bootstrap

modules.module_comment:
Bootstrapped app server factory for integration tests.

Creates a keeper account, API token, and signed session cookie on a
database. By default uses a cached in-memory PGlite (shared WASM instance
per vitest worker thread via `test_db.ts` module cache); pass `db` to use
an existing database (any driver) instead.

Also provides `create_test_app` — a combined helper that creates both
a `TestAppServer` and a fully assembled Hono app with middleware and routes.

declarations.name: resolve_fixture_path
declarations.doc_comment: Resolve an absolute path relative to the caller's module.

declarations.name: assert_surface_matches_snapshot
declarations.doc_comment:
Compare live surface against a committed snapshot JSON file.

Failure message instructs the developer to run `gro gen` to update the
snapshot — every fuz_app consumer wires the snapshot through a `*.gen.ts`
file so regeneration goes through the same pipeline as the rest of the
generated artifacts.

throws.description: if the live surface does not deep-equal the snapshot,

parameters.name: snapshot_path
parameters.description: absolute path to the committed JSON snapshot

declarations.name: assert_surface_deterministic
declarations.doc_comment: Verify surface generation is deterministic (build twice, compare).

declarations.name: assert_only_expected_public_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Bidirectional check: no unexpected public routes, no missing expected ones.

throws.description: if the live surface has public routes not in

declarations.doc_comment: Look up the merged error schema for a route+status from a pre-built schema lookup.

the Zod schema for that route+status, or `undefined` when no error
schema is declared for the status code

declarations.name: assert_error_schema_valid
declarations.doc_comment:
Assert that an error schema exists for a route+status and validate the body against it.

Protected routes should always have auto-derived error schemas (401 for authenticated,
403 for role-restricted). A missing schema indicates a gap in error schema derivation.

throws.description: if no schema is declared for the route+status pair.
if the body does not satisfy the declared schema.

declarations.name: assert_full_middleware_stack
declarations.doc_comment: Verify every route under a path prefix has the exact expected middleware stack.

throws.description: if no routes match `path_prefix`, or if any matching

declarations.type_signature: (surface: AppSurface, path_prefix: string, expected_middleware: string[]): void

modules.module_comment:
Assertion helpers for auth attack surface testing.

Plain functions called inside explicit `test()` blocks to verify
surface snapshots, public routes, and middleware stacks.

declarations.name: AdversarialTestOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for adversarial test runners (auth enforcement and input validation).

properties.name: build
properties.doc_comment: Build the app surface bundle (surface + route specs + middleware specs).

properties.name: roles
properties.doc_comment: All roles in the app (e.g. `['admin', 'keeper']`).

declarations.name: describe_adversarial_auth
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate adversarial HTTP auth enforcement test suites.

Describe blocks:
- unauthenticated → 401 — every protected route
- wrong role → 403 — every role route, tested with all non-matching roles
- authenticated without role → 403 — every role route, no-role context
- correct auth passes guard — every protected route, assert not 401/403

declarations.name: resolve_standard_error_schema_tightness
declarations.doc_comment:
Merge a consumer's `error_schema_tightness` option with
`default_error_schema_tightness` so `allowlist` and `ignore_statuses` are
additive rather than replacing.

- `undefined` → return the default as-is.
- `null` → return `null` (opt out of the assertion).
- object → spread the default, then consumer overrides for scalar fields
  (`min_specificity`), then concat stock-then-consumer for the list fields
  (`allowlist`, `ignore_statuses`) so consumer entries extend rather than
  replace.

Exported for direct use when a consumer calls `assert_error_schema_tightness`
outside the standard suite but still wants the additive merge.

declarations.name: StandardAttackSurfaceOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for the standard attack surface test suite.

properties.name: build
properties.doc_comment: Build the app surface bundle (surface + route specs + middleware specs).

properties.name: snapshot_path
properties.doc_comment: Absolute path to the committed snapshot JSON file.

properties.name: expected_public_routes
properties.doc_comment: Expected public routes, e.g. `['GET /health', 'POST /api/account/login']`.

properties.name: expected_api_middleware
properties.doc_comment: Expected middleware names for API routes, e.g. `['origin', 'session', 'request_context', 'bearer_auth']`.

properties.name: roles
properties.doc_comment: All roles in the app (e.g. `['admin', 'keeper']`).

properties.name: api_path_prefix
properties.doc_comment: Path prefix for middleware stack assertion. Default `'/api/'`.

properties.name: security_policy
properties.doc_comment: Security policy configuration. Omit for sensible defaults.

properties.name: error_schema_tightness
properties.doc_comment:
Error schema tightness assertion config. Defaults to
`default_error_schema_tightness` (ignores 401/403/429,
`min_specificity: 'enum'`, allowlist seeded with
`fuz_app_stock_route_tightness_allowlist`).

Consumer-supplied `allowlist` and `ignore_statuses` are **additive** —
the suite merges them underneath the stock defaults, so project-specific
entries don't need to re-list fuz_app's own stock routes. Pass a narrower
config to extend either list or tighten `min_specificity`; pass `null`
to skip the assertion and keep the audit log informational-only.

declarations.name: describe_standard_attack_surface_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Run the standard attack surface test suite.

Test groups:
1. Snapshot — live surface matches committed JSON
2. Determinism — building twice yields identical results
3. Public routes — bidirectional check (no unexpected, no missing)
4. Middleware stack — every API route has the full middleware chain
5. Surface invariants — structural assertions over `surface.routes` (error schemas, descriptions, duplicates, consistency)
6. RPC/WS surface invariants — structural assertions over `surface.rpc_endpoints` + `surface.ws_endpoints` (descriptions, protocol-action spread, kind ⇔ auth)
7. Security policy — rate limiting on sensitive routes, no unexpected public mutations, method conventions
8. Error schema tightness — informational log of generic vs specific error schemas, plus assertion against `default_error_schema_tightness` by default (opt out with `error_schema_tightness: null`)
9. Adversarial auth — unauthenticated/wrong-role/correct-auth enforcement
10. Adversarial input — input body and params validation
11. Adversarial 404 — stub 404 handlers, validate response bodies against declared schemas

Consumer test files call this with project-specific options, then add
any project-specific assertions in additional `describe` blocks.

modules.module_comment:
Adversarial auth enforcement test runners and the standard attack surface suite.

The combinatorial test runner (`describe_adversarial_auth`) generates
test suites for routes x auth levels. The standard suite
(`describe_standard_attack_surface_tests`) composes all attack surface
test groups into a single call.

Stubs, app factories, and assertion helpers live in focused submodules:
- `test_auth_stubs` — stub factories and pre-built dep bundles
- `test_auth_apps` — auth-level test app factories
- `test_auth_assertions` — snapshot, public route, and middleware assertions

declarations.name: AuditCompletenessTestOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Configuration for `describe_audit_completeness_tests`.

properties.name: setup_test
properties.doc_comment:
Per-test fixture-producing function. The audit suite calls this
in every `test()` body — `auth_integration_truncate_tables` clears
the audit log between tests, so each test re-bootstraps the
keeper and the observer admin against a fresh table.

properties.name: surface_source
properties.doc_comment:
App surface (with route specs). Constructed in TS by the consumer;
same shape for in-process and cross-process tests. The audit suite is
Tier 2 today (stays in-process per the cross-backend-integration
design), but takes the same surface shape as Tier 1 suites for
options uniformity.

properties.name: capabilities
properties.doc_comment: Backend capability declarations.

properties.name: session_options
properties.doc_comment: Session config — needed for factory-form rpc_endpoints resolution.

properties.name: rpc_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
RPC endpoint specs — required. The admin role_grant flow is RPC-only
and the suite hard-fails without it.

declarations.name: describe_audit_completeness_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Composable audit log completeness test suite.

Verifies that every auth mutation route produces the correct audit log
event type. Exercises routes via HTTP requests against a real PGlite
database, then reads events back through the `audit_log_list` RPC
(the production observation path the admin UI consumes).

throws.description: at setup time when `options.rpc_endpoints` is empty — the

modules.module_comment:
Composable audit log completeness test suite.

Verifies that every auth mutation route produces the expected audit log
event. Uses the real middleware stack and database, then **reads back
through the `audit_log_list` RPC** — the production observation path the
admin UI consumes. This is intentional end-to-end coverage: emit →
persist → query → wire response, all in one round-trip.

The trade is a deliberate transport coupling: a regression in
`audit_log_list_action_spec`'s auth or response shape can surface here as
a secondary failure. `describe_rpc_round_trip_tests` covers that RPC
directly, so primary breakages localize there first. For *unit-level*
"did the handler emit?" assertions without the persistence path, use
`create_recording_audit_emitter` from `audit_drift_guard.ts` — that
captures emits before they hit DB or transport.

Bootstrap is excluded because it requires filesystem token state that
`create_test_app` does not provide. Bootstrap audit logging is tested
separately in `bootstrap_account.db.test.ts`.

declarations.name: install_audit_drift_guard
declarations.doc_comment:
Register per-test `beforeEach` + `afterEach` hooks that catch any audit
emission with a metadata shape that fails its `audit_metadata_schemas`
entry, or an `event_type` not present in the active `AuditLogConfig`.

The production validation in `query_audit_log` is fail-open — it bumps
process-wide counters and proceeds, so a regression that emits an
undeclared metadata field or a typo'd event-type lands a row that
passes downstream queries but breaks forensics. Tests that exercise
audit emits should fail loudly when this happens.

Call at the top of every `describe` / `describe_db` block that fires
audit writes through `deps.audit.emit`. Resets counters before each
test and asserts zero on completion.

Pair with `await_pending_effects: true` (the default for
`create_test_app`) so fire-and-forget audit writes have completed by
the time the after-each check observes counter state.

declarations.name: AuditEmitMarker
declarations.doc_comment:
Marker pushed into a shared sequence array by an emit-recording
`audit_factory`. Pair with `RecordedClose` from
`connection_closer_helpers.ts` to test close-vs-emit ordering at
handler call sites — see `create_emit_ordering_audit_factory` below.

declarations.name: RecordingAuditEmitter
declarations.doc_comment:
Pair returned by {@link create_recording_audit_emitter} — the
`AuditEmitter` to inject as `deps.audit`, plus the shared `calls`
array that records every captured emission. Both fields are live —
callers read `calls` after exercising the handler to assert on the
audit metadata shape.

declarations.name: create_recording_audit_emitter
declarations.doc_comment:
Build a no-op `AuditEmitter` that records every `emit`, `emit_pool`, and
`emit_role_grant_target` call into `calls` as an `AuditLogInput`. Use to
capture audit metadata shapes in unit tests (e.g. password change failure
outcome, role-grant create denial) without standing up the full PGlite +
`query_audit_log` pipeline.

**Capture scope — all four production fan-out shapes.**
`emit_role_grant_target` mirrors `create_audit_emitter`'s lift logic in
place — `actor_id` / `account_id` / `ip` are populated from `auth` + `ctx`
and the `event_type` / `outcome` / `target_*_id` / `metadata` fields
forward from the input envelope. Tests asserting on role-grant-shape
emissions read out of the same homogeneous `calls` array.
`notify` is a no-op; `on_event_chain` is an empty array.

`emit` AND `emit_pool` both append to `calls` so cleanup-sweep tests
(which use `emit_pool` exclusively — see `auth/cleanup.ts`) can also
read assertions off the same array.

Pass `calls_ref` to write into a caller-owned array (callers that
declared `const events: Array<AuditLogInput> = []` and want to keep
the reference). Omit to let the helper allocate a fresh array and
return it on the `calls` field of the result.

The returned emitter is deliberately NOT frozen — slots stay mutable
so a test can override one when it needs bespoke shape (e.g. an
`emit_pool` that throws on the first call). The production
`create_audit_emitter` freeze invariant exists to catch the
`patch_audit_emit_capture` hot-patch footgun against the
closure-captured `emit`; the recording emitter has no inner closure,
so the freeze isn't load-bearing here.

declarations.name: create_emit_ordering_audit_factory
declarations.doc_comment:
Build an `audit_factory` that produces a real `create_audit_emitter`
with its `emit` decorated to push a `{kind: 'emit', at: seq.value++}`
marker into a shared sequence + events array. Used by the close-vs-emit
ordering test to compose against a shared sequence counter (typically
`create_recording_closer(seq_ref)` capturing eager-close calls).

Pass the returned factory through `create_test_app({audit_factory: …})`
— the test backend invokes it with its constructed `{db, log}` and
lands the decorated emitter on `backend.deps.audit`. Production
handlers dereference `deps.audit.emit` at call time, so the decorator
sees every subsequent handler invocation. The underlying `emit` still
runs — the decorator records the call, it does not suppress side
effects.

**Scope — both `emit` and `emit_role_grant_target`.** The decorator
is captured by `emit_role_grant_target`'s closure inside
`create_audit_emitter` (and re-exposed as the outer `emit` slot), so
role-grant-shape emissions land in `events_ref` alongside bare `emit`
calls. `emit_pool` and `notify` are not decorated — they take
`AuditLogInput` / `AuditLogEvent` directly without going through
`emit`, so handler-side `emit_pool` writes (today only
`auth/cleanup.ts`) skip capture. Close-firing handlers all reach for
`emit` or `emit_role_grant_target`, so the ordering test sees them
regardless of which entry point a future refactor picks.

Optionally accept `extra_options` to thread `on_audit_event` /
`audit_log_config` into the inner emitter — useful when a test wants
both ordering capture and a real SSE/WS guard wired into the same
emitter chain.
declarations.type_signature: <E extends { kind: string; at: number; }>(seq_ref: { value: number; }, events_ref: (AuditEmitMarker | E)[], extra_options?: Omit<CreateAuditEmitterOptions, "db" | ... 1 more ... | "emit_decorator"> | undefined): AuditFactory

declarations.name: create_test_request_context
declarations.doc_comment: Create a mock `RequestContext` with optional role role_grant.

declarations.name: create_test_app_from_specs
declarations.doc_comment: Create a Hono test app from route specs with optional auth context.
declarations.type_signature: (route_specs: RouteSpec[], auth_ctx?: RequestContext | undefined, credential_type?: "session" | "api_token" | "daemon_token" | undefined): Hono<BlankEnv, BlankSchema, "/">

parameters.name: auth_ctx
parameters.description: optional request context to inject via middleware

declarations.name: AuthTestApps
declarations.doc_comment: Pre-built Hono apps for each auth level, shared across adversarial test suites.

declarations.name: create_auth_test_apps
declarations.doc_comment: Create one Hono test app per auth level.

declarations.name: select_auth_app
declarations.doc_comment: Select the Hono test app with correct auth for a route.

throws.description: if `auth.roles` names a role not present in `apps.by_role` —

declarations.type_signature: (apps: AuthTestApps, auth: { account: "none" | "optional" | "required"; actor: "none" | "optional" | "required"; roles?: readonly string[] | undefined; credential_types?: readonly string[] | undefined; }): Hono<...>

parameters.name: auth
parameters.type: { account: "none" | "optional" | "required"; actor: "none" | "optional" | "required"; roles?: readonly string[] | undefined; credential_types?: readonly string[] | undefined; }

declarations.name: resolve_test_path
declarations.doc_comment: Replace Hono route params (`:foo`) with dummy values for HTTP testing.

modules.module_comment:
Auth test app factories for adversarial testing.

Creates Hono test apps at each auth level (public, authenticated, keeper,
per-role) for use in adversarial auth enforcement and input validation tests.

declarations.name: BootstrapSuccessTestOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `describe_bootstrap_success_tests`.

properties.name: create_route_specs
properties.doc_comment: Same factory the consumer's production server uses.

properties.name: rpc_endpoints
properties.doc_comment: RPC endpoints — passed through to `create_app_server` for shape parity.

properties.name: bootstrap
properties.doc_comment:
Live bootstrap config — the suite drives `POST /bootstrap` against
`bootstrap.token_path`. The suite does NOT assert on `on_bootstrap`
callback invocation (Hono-coupled signature is in-process only);
assertions land on observable DB state.

properties.name: bootstrap_token
properties.doc_comment: Override the synthetic token text. Default deterministic.

declarations.name: describe_bootstrap_success_tests
declarations.doc_comment:
Run the bootstrap success-path test suite against the consumer's
production-shaped wiring.

modules.module_comment:
Bootstrap success-path suite for consumer projects.

Exercises `POST /bootstrap` against an empty DB (no pre-keeper, lock
unflipped) through the real `bootstrap_account` flow. Asserts on
observable state — account exists, `bootstrap_lock.bootstrapped` is
true, audit row emitted, response body shape — rather than
`on_bootstrap` callback invocation, so the suite stays cross-impl
friendly when cross-process testing wires it against a spawned
Rust backend.

Folded into `describe_standard_tests` with a `bootstrap.mode === 'live'`
silent-skip gate; consumers wiring live bootstrap pick up success-path
coverage by default.

declarations.name: RecordedClose
declarations.doc_comment:
Record of a single `ConnectionCloser` method invocation. `at` is the
value of a monotonically-increasing sequence counter at the time of
the call — pair with `create_emit_ordering_audit_factory` to record both
close + audit emit calls into the same sequence for ordering tests.

declarations.name: create_recording_closer
declarations.doc_comment:
Build a `ConnectionCloser` that records every call into `calls` rather
than touching real transports. Each method returns 1 ("one socket
closed") regardless of whether a real socket exists — handlers
typically ignore the return value.

Pass `seq_ref` to share the sequence counter with a sibling
`create_emit_ordering_audit_factory` so tests can pin close-vs-emit
ordering at the handler call site. Without `seq_ref`, the closer
uses a fresh internal counter — `at: N` values within a single test
are meaningful, but cannot be compared against audit emit ordering.

declarations.name: assert_close_call
declarations.doc_comment:
Pin `{method, id}` on a single recorded close call without baking in
the `at: N` sequence number. Use at every "did the closer fire?"
assertion site; the sequence number is only meaningful for dedicated
ordering tests (paired with `create_emit_ordering_audit_factory`).

Throws via `assert.ok` if `call` is `undefined` — index a recorded
`calls` array directly (`calls[0]`) and let this helper handle the
missing-element case.
declarations.type_signature: (call: RecordedClose | undefined, method: "account" | "token" | "session", id: string): void

declarations.name: BackendBootstrapConfig
declarations.doc_comment:
Auth-bootstrap configuration for a spawnable test binary. The runner
writes `token` to `token_path` before launching the child, then POSTs
`bootstrap_path` (default `/api/account/bootstrap`) with the token plus
the `username` / `password` to mint the keeper account and capture the
session cookie. After health-probe, the runner reads
`daemon_token_path` to load the binary's deterministic daemon token,
which `default_cross_process_setup` threads onto the per-test
`TestFixture` for `_testing_reset` calls and other keeper-credential
operations.

properties.name: token_path
properties.doc_comment: Path the binary reads for the bootstrap token (env: `*_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN_PATH`).

properties.name: token
properties.doc_comment: Token text written to `token_path` before spawn.

properties.name: username
properties.doc_comment: Username for the bootstrapped keeper.

properties.name: password
properties.doc_comment: Password for the bootstrapped keeper.

properties.name: daemon_token_path
properties.doc_comment:
Path the test binary writes its daemon-token JSON to on boot
(env: `*_DAEMON_TOKEN_PATH`). `spawn_backend` reads this file once
after the health probe succeeds and threads the token onto
`BackendHandle.daemon_token` for `_testing_reset` calls plus any
other admin/keeper-gated cross-process tests.

declarations.name: BackendConfig
declarations.doc_comment:
Configuration for one spawnable test backend. Consumer factories
(`deno_backend_config()`, `rust_backend_config()`) produce these and
the runner consumes them through `spawn_backend`.

Path defaults match the standard fuz_app surface — Deno + Rust spine
(`zzz_server`, `zap_server`, `testing_spine_stub`) all converge on
`/api/account/{bootstrap,login,logout,password}`,
`/api/rpc`, `/api/ws`, `/health`. Override only when a backend
deliberately diverges (which it shouldn't, per the contract).

properties.name: name
properties.doc_comment: Diagnostic label (`"deno"`, `"rust"`, `"spine_stub"`). Surfaces in test output.

properties.name: start_command
properties.doc_comment: argv passed to the spawn. The first entry is the binary path.

properties.name: base_url
properties.doc_comment: Base URL for HTTP requests, including port (e.g. `http://localhost:8788`).

properties.name: rpc_path
properties.doc_comment: JSON-RPC endpoint mount point. Default `/api/rpc`.

properties.name: ws_path
properties.doc_comment: WebSocket endpoint mount point. Default `/api/ws`.

properties.name: health_path
properties.doc_comment: Readiness probe path. Default `/health`.

properties.name: bootstrap_path
properties.doc_comment: Bootstrap POST path. Default `/api/account/bootstrap`.

properties.name: cookie_name
properties.doc_comment:
Session cookie name the backend issues. Default `fuz_session` per
the ecosystem convergence; consumers using a custom session name
(legacy `zzz_session`, etc.) override. `default_cross_process_setup`
extracts the per-account session value from the transport jar by
this name so the cross-process `TestAccount.session_cookie` matches
the in-process shape.

properties.name: startup_timeout_ms
properties.doc_comment: How long to wait for the health probe (ms) before giving up.

properties.name: env
properties.doc_comment:
Env vars merged into the child process. Must include the binary's
`*_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN_PATH` + `*_DAEMON_TOKEN_PATH` env var names so
the binary reads/writes the right files. Also must include the
binary's `*_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` (typically
`'http://localhost:*'` for cross-process tests).

properties.name: bootstrap
properties.doc_comment: Auth bootstrap details — see `BackendBootstrapConfig`.

properties.name: capabilities
properties.doc_comment:
Capabilities this backend supports — drives `test_if(capabilities.X, ...)`
gating in suite bodies. See `capabilities.ts` for the vocabulary and
existing flags. Cross-process backends set `in_process_only: false`.

modules.module_comment:
Cross-process backend configuration.

`BackendConfig` describes a spawnable test binary — argv, mount paths,
env vars, bootstrap credentials, daemon-token discovery path, declared
capabilities. Consumer projects ship per-backend factories
(`deno_backend_config()`, `rust_backend_config()`,
`spine_stub_backend_config()`) that produce this shape; `spawn_backend`
consumes it.

fuz_app ships `spine_stub_backend_config()` as a convenience preset
(operational dep on `testing_spine_stub` — path-based discovery, no
`package.json` coupling to private_fuz). Otherwise backend-specific
knowledge (binary paths, port choices, env vars) is a consumer
concern; fuz_app's testing library knows nothing about Deno, Cargo, or
any specific runtime beyond that preset.

declarations.name: bootstrap_backend
declarations.doc_comment:
Spawn the test binary described by `config`, bootstrap a keeper, and
return the enriched handle.

The keeper transport is constructed against `config.base_url` with no
initial cookies; `bootstrap()` populates its jar with the session
cookie returned by `POST {config.bootstrap_path}`. Subsequent calls
against `bootstrapped.keeper_transport` are authenticated as keeper.

Mirrors the composition `default_cross_process_setup`'s caller would
otherwise hand-roll in every consumer's `globalSetup`.

declarations.name: TestBackendPaths
declarations.doc_comment:
Generic per-backend paths every cross-process test binary needs.
Consumers extend this with their own domain paths.

- `root` — the per-backend subtree under `os.tmpdir()`. Compose
  consumer-specific paths under here.
- `bootstrap_token_path` — `FUZ_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN_PATH`; harness writes
  the bootstrap token here before spawn.
- `daemon_token_path` — where `init_daemon_token` (Rust) and the TS
  server's daemon-token writer land the token (under `{root}/run/`).

declarations.name: build_test_backend_paths
declarations.doc_comment:
Build the generic path layout for a cross-process test backend.
`prefix` is typically the `BackendConfig.name` (e.g. `'deno'`,
`'rust'`, `'spine_stub'`).

modules.module_comment:
Per-backend filesystem layout under `os.tmpdir()` for cross-process
tests.

Isolation matters because vitest projects can run in parallel — a
shared `root` would mix daemon tokens across concurrently-running
backends. Each backend gets its own subtree via the `prefix` arg
(typically the `BackendConfig.name`).

Consumers compose: take the generic paths from
`build_test_backend_paths(name)`, add domain-specific dirs (e.g.
`zzz_dir`, `scoped_dir`) under the returned `root`.

declarations.name: BackendCapabilities
declarations.doc_comment:
Optional behaviors a backend may support. Each flag's TSDoc names the
tests that gate on it; add a new flag here before referencing it from
a suite body, and document the gating tests inline.

properties.name: bearer_auth
properties.doc_comment:
Bearer token auth (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`) is wired through
the backend's middleware stack. Gates the bearer-token cases in
`describe_standard_integration_tests` and `describe_rate_limiting_tests`.

properties.name: trusted_proxy
properties.doc_comment:
Trusted-proxy XFF parsing is wired (`X-Forwarded-For` etc.). Gates
the proxy-resolution cases in `describe_standard_integration_tests`
and the future cross-process proxy integration suite.

properties.name: login_rate_limit
properties.doc_comment:
Per-account login rate limiting is wired. Gates the per-account
rate-limit cases in `describe_rate_limiting_tests`.

properties.name: ws
properties.doc_comment:
WebSocket transport is reachable end-to-end. Gates the cross-process
WS round-trip suite; the in-process `describe_ws_round_trip_tests`
runs against `register_action_ws` directly and ignores this flag.

properties.name: sse
properties.doc_comment:
SSE transport is reachable end-to-end. Gates the cross-process SSE
close-detection cases; in-process SSE uses the
`on_audit_event` hook and ignores this flag.

properties.name: in_process_only
properties.doc_comment:
Test has direct access to backend-internal state (keyring for
signing cookies, DB pool for FK-structural raw queries). Always
`true` for in-process Hono via `default_in_process_setup`; always
`false` cross-process. Gates the 3 keyring reads in
`describe_standard_integration_tests` (expired-cookie generation)
and the FK-structural raw query in `describe_audit_completeness_tests`.

declarations.name: in_process_capabilities
declarations.doc_comment:
Capability declarations for the in-process Hono transport. Every flag
is `true` because in-process testing exercises the full backend with
no missing optional behaviors. Cross-process consumers
declare each flag explicitly per backend.

declarations.name: test_if
declarations.doc_comment:
Conditional `test()` wrapper — registers a vitest case only when
`cond` is true; otherwise registers it as `.skip` so the run still
surfaces the gated coverage in the report.

Thin wrapper around vitest's `test.skipIf(!cond)` with the argument
order flipped to match the more readable `test_if(capabilities.bearer_auth, ...)`
call pattern.
declarations.type_signature: (cond: boolean, name: string, fn: () => void | Promise<void>): void

modules.module_comment:
Capability vocabulary for cross-backend integration testing.

Backends declare which optional behaviors they support; suite bodies
call `test_if(capabilities.X, ...)` to skip cases the backend doesn't
implement. No `if (config.name === 'rust')` branches anywhere — name-
checking is a code smell that says capability vocabulary is missing.

In-process Hono via `default_in_process_setup` declares every
capability `true` (see `in_process_capabilities`). Cross-process
backends opt in per-flag on their `BackendConfig`.

declarations.name: ts_default_capabilities
declarations.doc_comment:
Capabilities shared by TS-family backends — same canonical
implementation, same feature set. No trusted-proxy phase (the test
binary doesn't enable proxy parsing) and no per-account login rate
limit (the TS canonical path leaves the limiter null in test mode).

declarations.name: rust_default_capabilities
declarations.doc_comment:
Capabilities for the Rust family. Adds `trusted_proxy: true` (the
Rust spine's client-IP middleware is always wired; the env-gate just
controls whether XFF is consulted vs the TCP peer IP) and
`login_rate_limit: true` (env-gated bucket on `/login` + `/password`).

properties.name: name
properties.doc_comment: Diagnostic label; also used as the tmpdir prefix when `paths` is omitted.

properties.name: port
properties.doc_comment: TCP port the binary listens on.

properties.name: start_command
properties.doc_comment: argv passed to the spawn (first entry is the binary).

properties.name: database_url
properties.doc_comment: Defaults to `'memory://'` (in-memory PGlite).

properties.name: extra_env
properties.doc_comment: Merged on top of the generic env baseline; later keys win.

properties.name: capabilities
properties.doc_comment: Defaults to `ts_default_capabilities`.

properties.name: paths
properties.doc_comment: Pre-computed paths; defaults to `build_test_backend_paths(name)`.

properties.name: bootstrap_overrides
properties.doc_comment: Override individual bootstrap fields (username/password/token).

properties.name: port_env_var
properties.doc_comment:
Env-var name the binary reads for its port. Defaults to `'PORT'`.
Consumers whose binary reads a different name (e.g. `'ZZZ_PORT'`)
override.

declarations.name: make_default_ts_backend_config
declarations.doc_comment:
